revision.text:
'''野中古墳'''（のなかこふん）は、[[大阪府]][[藤井寺市]]野中３丁目に所在する[[古墳時代]]中期の[[方墳]]である。鉄製の武器、武具、[[須恵器]]など大量の遺物が出土し、国の[[史跡]]に指定されている。
== 概要== [[墓山古墳]]の後円部北側に位置し、同古墳の[[陪塚]]とも推定されている・墳丘は２段に築成されており、一辺37ｍ、高さ４ｍを測る。周濠があり、[[葺石]]、[[円筒埴輪]]列、[[衣笠]]、衝角付冑、囲形などの[[形象埴輪]]が存在し、墳頂部からは大量の須恵器、[[土師器]]が出土している。墳頂部地下には組合式[[木棺]]と木櫃からなる内部構造が5列に並列して埋納されており、人体埋葬が考えられるのは第2列の木棺のみで、他の４列はすべて、副葬品埋納施設と推定される。それらの施設からは[[短甲]]、冑、草摺、肩甲などの鉄製武具、鉄刀、鉄剣、鉄鏃などの大量の武器が出土している。大量の鉄製品、須恵質土器、武器・武具の副葬形態など中期古墳の研究の上で欠くことのできない内容の古墳である。5世紀前半から中葉頃の築造と考えられる。
== 文献 == 『日本古墳大辞典』 株式会社東京堂出版 １９８９年 [[category:古墳]] [[category:大阪府の古墳]] [[category:藤井寺市]]

'''野中古墳'''（のなかこふん）は、[[大阪府]][[藤井寺市]]野中3丁目に所在する[[古墳時代]]中期の古墳（[[方墳]]）である。鉄製の武器、武具、[[須恵器]]など大量の遺物が出土し、国の[[史跡]]に指定されている。
== 概要== [[墓山古墳]]の後円部北側に位置し、同古墳の[[陪塚]]とも推定されている・墳丘は2段に築成されており、一辺37m、高さ4m。周濠があり、[[葺石]]、[[円筒埴輪]]列、[[衣笠]]、衝角付冑、囲形などの[[形象埴輪]]が存在し、墳頂部からは大量の須恵器、[[土師器]]が出土している。墳頂部地下には組合式[[木棺]]と木櫃からなる内部構造が5列に並列して埋納されており、人体埋葬が考えられるのは第2列の木棺のみで、他の4列はすべて、副葬品埋納施設と推定される。それらの施設からは[[短甲]]、冑、草摺、肩甲などの鉄製武具、鉄刀、鉄剣、鉄鏃などの大量の武器が出土している。大量の鉄製品、須恵質土器、武器・武具の副葬形態など中期古墳の研究の上で欠くことのできない内容の古墳である。5世紀前半から中葉頃の築造と考えられている。
== 文献 == 『日本古墳大辞典』 株式会社東京堂出版 1989年
== 外部リンク == *[http://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/10kakuka/34shakaikyoiku/03bunkazai/04isekishokai/13kaisetsu/k_nonaka.html 野中古墳 （のなかこふん）]（羽曳野市ウェブサイト）
{{デフォルトソート:のなかこふん}} [[Category:方墳]] [[Category:大阪府にある国指定の史跡]] [[category:大阪府の古墳]] [[category:藤井寺市]]

{{日本の古墳 |名称= 野中古墳 |画像= |所在地= 大阪府藤井寺市野中 |位置= |形状= 方墳 |規模=一辺37ｍ |築造年代= 5世紀 |埋葬施設=木棺 木櫃 |被葬者= |出土品=甲冑 鉄製武器 |史跡指定=国の[[史跡]] |特記事項= }}
'''野中古墳'''（のなかこふん）は、[[大阪府]][[藤井寺市]]野中3丁目に所在する[[古墳時代]]中期の古墳（[[方墳]]）である。鉄製の武器、武具、[[須恵器]]など大量の遺物が出土した。「[[古市古墳群]]」の一部として国の[[史跡]]に指定されている。
== 概要== [[古市古墳群]]に属し、[[墓山古墳]]の後円部北側に位置しており同古墳の[[陪塚]]とも推定されている。墳丘は2段に築成されており、一辺37m、高さ4m。周濠があり、[[葺石]]、[[円筒埴輪]]列、蓋（きぬがさ）、衝角付冑、囲形などの[[形象埴輪]]が存在し、墳頂部からは大量の須恵器、[[土師器]]が出土している。墳頂部地下には組合式[[木棺]]と木櫃からなる内部主体が5列に並列して埋納されており、人体埋葬が考えられるのは第2列の木棺のみで、他の4列はすべて、副葬品埋納施設と推定される。それらの施設からは[[短甲]]11領、冑11箇のほか、、草摺、肩甲などの鉄製武具、鉄刀、鉄剣、鉄鏃などの大量の武器が出土している。大量の鉄製品、須恵質土器、武器・武具の副葬形態など中期古墳の研究の上で欠くことのできない内容の古墳である。5世紀前半から中葉頃の築造と考えられている。
1995年に国の史跡に指定。2001年には既指定の他の古墳と統合指定され、国の史跡「古市古墳群」の一部となっている。甲冑等の出土品は大阪大学に所蔵されている。
== 文献 == 『日本古墳大辞典』 株式会社東京堂出版 1989年

'''野中古墳'''（のなかこふん）は、[[大阪府]][[藤井寺市]]野中3丁目に所在する[[古墳時代]]中期の古墳（[[方墳]]）である。鉄製の武器、武具、[[須恵器]]など大量の遺物が出土した。「[[古市古墳群]]」の一部として国の[[史跡]]に指定されている。
== 概要== [[古市古墳群]]に属し、[[墓山古墳]]の後円部北側に位置しており同古墳の[[陪塚]]とも推定されている。墳丘は2段に築成されており、一辺37m、高さ4m。周濠があり、[[葺石]]、[[円筒埴輪]]列、蓋（きぬがさ）、衝角付冑、囲形などの[[形象埴輪]]が存在し、墳頂部からは大量の須恵器、[[土師器]]が出土している。墳頂部地下には組合式[[木棺]]と木櫃からなる内部主体が5列に並列して埋納されており、人体埋葬が考えられるのは第2列の木棺のみで、他の4列はすべて、副葬品埋納施設と推定される。それらの施設からは[[短甲]]11領、冑11箇のほか、草摺、肩甲などの鉄製武具、鉄刀、鉄剣、鉄鏃などの大量の武器が出土している。大量の鉄製品、須恵質土器、武器・武具の副葬形態など中期古墳の研究の上で欠くことのできない内容の古墳である。5世紀前半から中葉頃の築造と考えられている。
1995年に国の史跡に指定。2001年には既指定の他の古墳と統合指定され、国の史跡「古市古墳群」の一部となっている。甲冑等の出土品は大阪大学に所蔵されている。
== 文献 == 『日本古墳大辞典』 株式会社東京堂出版 1989年
== 関連項目==

== 外部リンク == *[http://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/10kakuka/34shakaikyoiku/03bunkazai/04isekishokai/13kaisetsu/k_nonaka.html 野中古墳 （のなかこふん）]（羽曳野市ウェブサイト）

== 概要== [[古市古墳群]]に属し、[[墓山古墳]]の後円部北側に位置しており同古墳の[[陪塚]]とも推定されている。墳丘は2段に築成されており、一辺37m、高さ4m。周濠があり、[[葺石]]、[[円筒埴輪]]列、蓋（きぬがさ）、衝角付冑、囲形などの[[形象埴輪]]が存在し、墳頂部からは大量の須恵器、[[土師器]]が出土している。墳頂部地下には組合式[[木棺]]と木櫃からなる内部主体が5列に並列して埋納されており、人体埋葬が考えられるのは第2列の木棺のみで、他の4列はすべて、副葬品埋納施設と推定される。それらの施設からは[[短甲]]11領、冑11箇のほか、草摺、肩甲などの鉄製武具、鉄刀、鉄剣、鉄鏃などの大量の武器が出土している。大量の鉄製品、須恵質土器、武器・武具の副葬形態など中期古墳の研究の上で欠くことのできない内容の古墳である。5世紀前半から中葉頃の築造と考えられている。

== 外部リンク == *[http://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/10kakuka/34shakaikyoiku/03bunkazai/04isekishokai/13kaisetsu/k_nonaka.html 野中古墳 （のなかこふん）]（羽曳野市ウェブサイト） *[http://www.let.osaka-u.ac.jp/kouko/nonaka/ 野中古墳]（大阪大学考古学研究室）
{{デフォルトソート:のなかこふん}} [[Category:方墳]] [[Category:大阪府にある国指定の史跡]] [[category:大阪府の古墳]] [[category:藤井寺市の歴史]] [[Category:古市古墳群]]

{{日本の古墳 |名称= 野中古墳 |画像= |所属=[[古市古墳群]] |所在地= 大阪府藤井寺市野中 |位置={{coord|34|33|36.9|N|135|36|9.3|E|region:JP-27}} |形状= 方墳 |規模=一辺37ｍ |築造年代= 5世紀 |埋葬施設=木棺 木櫃 |被葬者= |出土品=甲冑 鉄製武器 |指定文化財=国の[[史跡]] |特記事項= }}
'''野中古墳'''（のなかこふん）は、[[大阪府]][[藤井寺市]]野中3丁目に所在する[[古墳時代]]中期の古墳（[[方墳]]）である。鉄製の武器、武具、[[須恵器]]など大量の遺物が出土した。[[文化遺産 (世界遺産)|世界文化遺産]]の「[[古市古墳群]]」の一部として国の[[史跡]]に指定されている。
== 概要== [[古市古墳群]]に属し、[[墓山古墳]]の後円部北側に位置しており同古墳の[[陪塚]]とも推定されている。墳丘は2段に築成されており、一辺37m、高さ4m。周濠があり、[[葺石]]、[[円筒埴輪]]列、蓋（きぬがさ）、[[衝角付冑]]、囲形などの[[形象埴輪]]が存在し、墳頂部からは大量の[[須恵器]]、[[土師器]]が出土している。墳頂部地下には組合式[[木棺]]と木櫃からなる内部主体が5列に並列して埋納されており、人体埋葬が考えられるのは第2列の木棺のみで、他の4列はすべて、副葬品埋納施設と推定される。それらの施設からは[[短甲]]11領、[[冑]]11箇のほか、草摺、肩甲などの鉄製武具、[[直刀|鉄刀]]、[[剣|鉄剣]]、[[鉄鏃]]などの大量の武器が出土している。大量の鉄製品、須恵質土器、武器・武具の副葬形態など中期古墳の研究の上で欠くことのできない内容の古墳である。5世紀前半から中葉頃の築造と考えられている。
1995年に国の史跡に指定。2001年には既指定の他の古墳と統合指定され、国の史跡「古市古墳群」の一部となっている。[[甲冑]]等の出土品は大阪大学に所蔵されており、2015年に国の重要文化財に指定されている。
== 文献 == 『日本古墳大辞典』 株式会社東京堂出版 1989年
== 関連項目==
*[[古市古墳群]] *[[野中アリ山古墳]]
== 外部リンク == *[http://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/10kakuka/34shakaikyoiku/03bunkazai/04isekishokai/13kaisetsu/k_nonaka.html 野中古墳 （のなかこふん）]（羽曳野市ウェブサイト） *[http://www.let.osaka-u.ac.jp/kouko/nonaka/ 野中古墳]（大阪大学考古学研究室）
{{デフォルトソート:のなかこふん}} [[Category:方墳]] [[Category:大阪府にある国指定の史跡]] [[category:大阪府の古墳]] [[category:藤井寺市の歴史]] [[Category:古市古墳群]]

{{日本の古墳 |名称= 野中古墳 |画像={{画像募集中|cat=藤井寺市}} |所属=[[古市古墳群]] |所在地=大阪府藤井寺市野中 |位置={{Coord|34|33|36.9|N|135|36|9.3|E|region:JP-27}} |形状=方墳 |規模=一辺37m |築造年代=5世紀 |埋葬施設=木棺 木櫃 |被葬者= |出土品=甲冑 鉄製武器 |指定文化財=国の[[史跡]] |特記事項= }} '''野中古墳'''（のなかこふん）は、[[大阪府]][[藤井寺市]]野中3丁目に所在する[[古墳時代]]中期の古墳（[[方墳]]）である。鉄製の武器、武具、[[須恵器]]など大量の遺物が出土した。[[文化遺産 (世界遺産)|世界文化遺産]]の「[[古市古墳群]]」の一部として国の[[史跡]]に指定されている。
== 概要== [[古市古墳群]]に属し、[[墓山古墳]]の後円部北側に位置しており同古墳の[[陪塚]]とも推定されている。墳丘は2段に築成されており、一辺37m、高さ4m。周濠があり、[[葺石]]、[[円筒埴輪]]列、蓋（きぬがさ）、[[衝角付冑]]、囲形などの[[形象埴輪]]が存在し、墳頂部からは大量の[[須恵器]]、[[土師器]]が出土している。墳頂部地下には組合式[[木棺]]と木櫃からなる内部主体が5列に並列して埋納されており、人体埋葬が考えられるのは第2列の木棺のみで、他の4列はすべて、副葬品埋納施設と推定される。それらの施設からは[[短甲]]11領、[[冑]]11箇のほか、草摺、肩甲などの鉄製武具、[[直刀|鉄刀]]、[[剣|鉄剣]]、[[鉄鏃]]などの大量の武器が出土している。大量の鉄製品、須恵質土器、武器・武具の副葬形態など中期古墳の研究の上で欠くことのできない内容の古墳である。5世紀前半から中葉頃の築造と考えられている。
1995年に国の史跡に指定。2001年には既指定の他の古墳と統合指定され、国の史跡「古市古墳群」の一部となっている。[[甲冑]]等の出土品は大阪大学に所蔵されており、2015年に国の重要文化財に指定されている。
== 文献 == * 『日本古墳大辞典』、株式会社東京堂出版、1989年。
== 関連項目 == *[[古市古墳群]] *[[野中アリ山古墳]] * [[カトンボ山古墳]] - 野中古墳と同様に多量の滑石製模造品・臼玉が埋納された陪塚。
== 外部リンク == *[http://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/10kakuka/34shakaikyoiku/03bunkazai/04isekishokai/13kaisetsu/k_nonaka.html 野中古墳（のなかこふん）]（羽曳野市ウェブサイト） *[http://www.let.osaka-u.ac.jp/kouko/nonaka/ 野中古墳]（大阪大学考古学研究室）

{{日本の古墳 |名称= 野中古墳 |画像={{画像募集中|cat=藤井寺市}} |所属=[[古市古墳群]] |所在地=大阪府藤井寺市野中 |位置={{Coord|34|33|36.9|N|135|36|9.3|E|region:JP-27}} |形状=方墳 |規模=一辺37m |築造年代=5世紀 |埋葬施設=木棺 木櫃 |被葬者= |出土品=甲冑 鉄製武器 |指定文化財=国の[[史跡]] |特記事項= }} '''野中古墳'''（のなかこふん）は、[[大阪府]][[藤井寺市]]野中3丁目に所在する[[古墳時代]]中期の古墳（[[方墳]]）である。鉄製の武器、武具、[[須恵器]]など大量の遺物が出土した。[[文化遺産 (世界遺産)|世界文化遺産]]の「[[古市古墳群]]」の一部として国の[[史跡]]に指定されている。
== 概要== [[古市古墳群]]に属し、[[墓山古墳]]の後円部北側に位置しており同古墳の[[陪塚]]とも推定されている<ref name="yomiuri140212">『読売新聞』2014年2月12日朝刊文化面19頁「野中古墳 甲冑修復終える」（読売新聞東京本社）</ref>。墳丘は2段に築成された方墳であり、一辺37m{{R|yomiuri140212}}、高さ4m、周濠があり、[[葺石]]、[[円筒埴輪]]列、蓋（きぬがさ）、[[衝角付冑]]、囲形などの[[形象埴輪]]が存在し、墳頂部からは大量の[[須恵器]]、[[土師器]]が出土している{{R|yomiuri140212}}。墳頂部地下には組合式[[木棺]]と木櫃からなる内部主体が5列に並列して埋納されており、人体埋葬が考えられるのは第2列の木棺のみで、他の4列はすべて、副葬品埋納施設と推定される。それらの施設からは[[短甲]]11領、[[冑]]11箇のほか、草摺、肩甲などの鉄製武具、[[直刀|鉄刀]]、[[剣|鉄剣]]、[[鉄鏃]]などの大量の武器が出土している。大量の鉄製品、須恵質土器、武器・武具の副葬形態など中期古墳の研究の上で欠くことのできない内容の古墳である。5世紀前半から中葉頃の築造と考えられている。
1964年、[[大阪大学]]が実施した発掘調査により遺物約2000点が出土した<ref>『読売新聞』2018年12月22日朝刊大阪2面32頁「重文遺物3D化 前進 阪大計測計画 ネット募金 目標150万円達成＝大阪」（読売新聞大阪本社）</ref>。1995年に国の史跡に指定される。2001年、既指定の他の古墳と統合指定され、国の史跡「古市古墳群」の一部となっている。[[甲冑]]などの出土品は大阪大学に所蔵されており、2015年に国の重要文化財に指定されている<ref>『読売新聞』2015年3月15日朝刊市内面33頁「大阪市立美術館 国登録文化財に 審議会答申＝大阪」（読売新聞大阪本社）</ref>。なお、1964年に本遺跡の発掘調査を撮影したカラー映像が2009年に発見された<ref>『読売新聞』2012年12月24日朝刊河内面23頁「「百舌鳥・古市」野中古墳 48年前の発掘 鮮明映像＝大阪」（読売新聞大阪本社）</ref>。
== 文献 == * 『日本古墳大辞典』、株式会社東京堂出版、1989年。
== 関連項目 == * [[古市古墳群]] * [[野中アリ山古墳]] * [[カトンボ山古墳]] - 野中古墳と同様に多量の滑石製模造品・臼玉が埋納された陪塚。
== 外部リンク == *[http://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/10kakuka/34shakaikyoiku/03bunkazai/04isekishokai/13kaisetsu/k_nonaka.html 野中古墳（のなかこふん）]（羽曳野市ウェブサイト） *[http://www.let.osaka-u.ac.jp/kouko/nonaka/ 野中古墳]（大阪大学考古学研究室）

revision.comment: Created page with "'''Nonaka Kofun''' (野中古墳) is a mid-[[Kofun period]] burial mound ([[kofun]]) located in {{ill|Fujidera|ja|藤井寺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. A significant number of iron weapons, armor, and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} have been excavated from the site. The kofun is designated as a part of the [[UNESCO]] [[World Cultural Heritage]] "Ancient Tumulus Clusters," specifically within the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}}. It..."
revision.text:
'''Nonaka Kofun''' (野中古墳) is a mid-[[Kofun period]] burial mound ([[kofun]]) located in {{ill|Fujidera|ja|藤井寺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. A significant number of iron weapons, armor, and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} have been excavated from the site. The kofun is designated as a part of the [[UNESCO]] [[World Cultural Heritage]] "Ancient Tumulus Clusters," specifically within the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}}. It is also a [[National Historic Site of Japan]].
== Overview == The Nonaka Kofun is part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} and is situated north of the rear circle of the {{ill|Makasuyama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳}}, leading to speculation that it may have served as a {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|subsidiary burial mound}} for the latter.<ref name="yomiuri140212">"Restoration Completed for Nonaka Kofun Armor," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' February 12, 2014.</ref>
The mound is a square-shaped tumulus (方墳) built in two tiers, with each side measuring 37 meters and a height of 4 meters. It features a surrounding moat, {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (roofing stones), cylindrical haniwa rows, and figurative haniwa, including helmets, "Kinugasa" lids, and horned helmets (衝角付冑). Numerous {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} were excavated from the mound's summit.<ref name="yomiuri140212"/>
Beneath the mound's summit, five sets of burial facilities were aligned in parallel, consisting of wooden coffins and wooden chests. Only the second wooden coffin is believed to have contained human remains, while the other four are interpreted as dedicated for grave goods. These facilities yielded a significant collection of grave goods, including: * 11 sets of iron armor ([[tankō]] 短甲) * 11 helmets (冑) * Components such as grass skirts and shoulder guards * Iron swords (鉄刀), iron spears (鉄剣), and a large number of iron arrowheads (鉄鏃).
The abundant iron artifacts, Sue ware, and weapons provide invaluable insights into the burial practices of the mid-Kofun period. The tumulus is estimated to have been built during the early to mid-5th century.
== Excavations and Preservation == In 1964, an excavation led by [[Osaka University]] unearthed approximately 2,000 artifacts.<ref>"Crowdfunding Advances for 3D Digitalization of Important Artifacts," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' December 22, 2018.</ref> The site was designated as a National Historic Site in 1995 and incorporated into the unified designation of the "Furuichi Kofun Cluster" in 2001. The iron armor and other artifacts are stored at Osaka University and were designated as Important Cultural Properties of Japan in 2015.<ref>"Osaka City Art Museum Registered as Cultural Property," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' March 15, 2015.</ref>
Additionally, a 1964 color film documenting the excavation was rediscovered in 2009.<ref>"1964 Nonaka Kofun Excavation Film Found," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' December 24, 2012.</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Literature == * ''Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia,'' Tokyo-dō Shuppan, 1989.
== Related Sites == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} * {{ill|Nonaka Ariyama Kofun|ja|野中アリ山古墳}} * {{ill|Katombo-yama Kofun|ja|カトンボ山古墳}} - A baizuka featuring similar abundant burial goods, including imitation stone products and beads.
== External Links == * [http://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/10kakuka/34shakaikyoiku/03bunkazai/04isekishokai/13kaisetsu/k_nonaka.html Nonaka Kofun] (Habikino City Website) * [http://www.let.osaka-u.ac.jp/kouko/nonaka/ Nonaka Kofun] (Osaka University Archaeology Laboratory)

== Overview == The Nonaka Kofun is part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} and is situated north of the rear circle of the {{ill|Makasuyama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳}}, leading to speculation that it may have served as a {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|subsidiary burial mound}} for the latter.<ref name="yomiuri140212">"Restoration Completed for Nonaka Kofun Armor," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' February 12, 2014.</ref>
The mound is a square-shaped tumulus (方墳) built in two tiers, with each side measuring 37 meters and a height of 4 meters. It features a surrounding moat, {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (roofing stones), cylindrical haniwa rows, and figurative haniwa, including helmets, "Kinugasa" lids, and horned helmets (衝角付冑). Numerous {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} were excavated from the mound's summit.<ref name="yomiuri140212"/>
Beneath the mound's summit, five sets of burial facilities were aligned in parallel, consisting of wooden coffins and wooden chests. Only the second wooden coffin is believed to have contained human remains, while the other four are interpreted as dedicated for grave goods. These facilities yielded a significant collection of grave goods, including: * 11 sets of iron armor ([[tankō]] 短甲) * 11 helmets (冑) * Components such as grass skirts and shoulder guards * Iron swords (鉄刀), iron spears (鉄剣), and a large number of iron arrowheads (鉄鏃).
The abundant iron artifacts, Sue ware, and weapons provide invaluable insights into the burial practices of the mid-Kofun period. The tumulus is estimated to have been built during the early to mid-5th century.
== Excavations and Preservation == In 1964, an excavation led by [[Osaka University]] unearthed approximately 2,000 artifacts.<ref>"Crowdfunding Advances for 3D Digitalization of Important Artifacts," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' December 22, 2018.</ref> The site was designated as a National Historic Site in 1995 and incorporated into the unified designation of the "Furuichi Kofun Cluster" in 2001. The iron armor and other artifacts are stored at Osaka University and were designated as Important Cultural Properties of Japan in 2015.<ref>"Osaka City Art Museum Registered as Cultural Property," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' March 15, 2015.</ref>
Additionally, a 1964 color film documenting the excavation was rediscovered in 2009.<ref>"1964 Nonaka Kofun Excavation Film Found," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' December 24, 2012.</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Literature == * ''Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia,'' Tokyo-dō Shuppan, 1989.
== Related Sites == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} * {{ill|Nonaka Ariyama Kofun|ja|野中アリ山古墳}} * {{ill|Katombo-yama Kofun|ja|カトンボ山古墳}} - A baizuka featuring similar abundant burial goods, including imitation stone products and beads.

A significant number of iron weapons, armor, and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} have been excavated from the site. The kofun is designated as a part of the [[UNESCO]] [[World Cultural Heritage]] "Ancient Tumulus Clusters," specifically within the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}}. It is also a [[National Historic Site of Japan]].
== Overview == The Nonaka Kofun is part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} and is situated north of the rear circle of the {{ill|Makasuyama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳}}, leading to speculation that it may have served as a {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|subsidiary burial mound}} for the latter.<ref name="yomiuri140212">"Restoration Completed for Nonaka Kofun Armor," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' February 12, 2014.</ref>

Numerous {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} were excavated from the mound's summit.<ref name="yomiuri140212"/>
Beneath the mound's summit, five sets of burial facilities were aligned in parallel, consisting of wooden coffins and wooden chests. Only the second wooden coffin is believed to have contained human remains, while the other four are interpreted as dedicated for grave goods. These facilities yielded a significant collection of grave goods, including: * 11 sets of iron armor ([[tankō]] 短甲) * 11 helmets (冑) * Components such as grass skirts and shoulder guards * Iron swords (鉄刀), iron spears (鉄剣), and a large number of iron arrowheads (鉄鏃).

revision.text:
'''Nonaka Kofun''' (野中古墳) is a mid-[[Kofun period]] burial mound ([[kofun]]) located in {{ill|Fujidera|ja|藤井寺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. A significant number of iron weapons, armor, and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} have been excavated from the site. The kofun is designated as a part of the [[UNESCO]] [[World Cultural Heritage]] "Ancient Tumulus Clusters," specifically within the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}}. It is also a [[National Historic Site of Japan]].
== Overview == The Nonaka Kofun is part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} and is situated north of the rear circle of the {{ill|Makasuyama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳}}, leading to speculation that it may have served as a {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|subsidiary burial mound}} for the latter.<ref name="yomiuri140212">"Restoration Completed for Nonaka Kofun Armor," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' February 12, 2014.</ref>
The mound is a square-shaped tumulus (方墳) built in two tiers, with each side measuring 37 meters and a height of 4 meters. It features a surrounding moat, {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (roofing stones), cylindrical haniwa rows, and figurative haniwa, including helmets, "Kinugasa" lids, and horned helmets (衝角付冑). Numerous {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} were excavated from the mound's summit.<ref name="yomiuri140212"/>
Beneath the mound's summit, five sets of burial facilities were aligned in parallel, consisting of wooden coffins and wooden chests. Only the second wooden coffin is believed to have contained human remains, while the other four are interpreted as dedicated for grave goods. These facilities yielded a significant collection of grave goods, including: * 11 sets of iron armor ([[tankō]] 短甲) * 11 helmets (冑) * Components such as grass skirts and shoulder guards * Iron swords (鉄刀), iron spears (鉄剣), and a large number of iron arrowheads (鉄鏃).
The abundant iron artifacts, Sue ware, and weapons provide invaluable insights into the burial practices of the mid-Kofun period. The tumulus is estimated to have been built during the early to mid-5th century.
== Excavations and Preservation == In 1964, an excavation led by [[Osaka University]] unearthed approximately 2,000 artifacts.<ref>"Crowdfunding Advances for 3D Digitalization of Important Artifacts," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' December 22, 2018.</ref> The site was designated as a National Historic Site in 1995 and incorporated into the unified designation of the "Furuichi Kofun Cluster" in 2001. The iron armor and other artifacts are stored at Osaka University and were designated as Important Cultural Properties of Japan in 2015.<ref>"Osaka City Art Museum Registered as Cultural Property," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' March 15, 2015.</ref>
Additionally, a 1964 color film documenting the excavation was rediscovered in 2009.<ref>"1964 Nonaka Kofun Excavation Film Found," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' December 24, 2012.</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Literature == * ''Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia,'' Tokyo-dō Shuppan, 1989.
== Related Sites == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} * {{ill|Nonaka Ariyama Kofun|ja|野中アリ山古墳}} * {{ill|Katombo-yama Kofun|ja|カトンボ山古墳}} - A baizuka featuring similar abundant burial goods, including imitation stone products and beads.
== External Links == * [http://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/10kakuka/34shakaikyoiku/03bunkazai/04isekishokai/13kaisetsu/k_nonaka.html Nonaka Kofun] (Habikino City Website) * [http://www.let.osaka-u.ac.jp/kouko/nonaka/ Nonaka Kofun] (Osaka University Archaeology Laboratory)

The iron armor and other artifacts are stored at Osaka University and were designated as Important Cultural Properties of Japan in 2015.<ref>"Osaka City Art Museum Registered as Cultural Property," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' March 15, 2015.</ref>
Additionally, a 1964 color film documenting the excavation was rediscovered in 2009.<ref>"1964 Nonaka Kofun Excavation Film Found," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' December 24, 2012.</ref>

Only the second wooden coffin is believed to have contained human remains, while the other four are interpreted as dedicated for grave goods. These facilities yielded a significant collection of grave goods, including: * 11 sets of iron armor ([[tankō]] 短甲) * 11 helmets (冑) * Components such as grass skirts and shoulder guards * Iron swords (鉄刀), iron spears (鉄剣), and a large number of iron arrowheads (鉄鏃).
The abundant iron artifacts, Sue ware, and weapons provide invaluable insights into the burial practices of the mid-Kofun period. The tumulus is estimated to have been built during the early to mid-5th century.
== Excavations and Preservation == In 1964, an excavation led by [[Osaka University]] unearthed approximately 2,000 artifacts.<ref>"Crowdfunding Advances for 3D Digitalization of Important Artifacts," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' December 22, 2018.</ref> The site was designated as a National Historic Site in 1995 and incorporated into the unified designation of the "Furuichi Kofun Cluster" in 2001. The iron armor and other artifacts are stored at Osaka University and were designated as Important Cultural Properties of Japan in 2015.<ref>"Osaka City Art Museum Registered as Cultural Property," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' March 15, 2015.</ref>
Additionally, a 1964 color film documenting the excavation was rediscovered in 2009.<ref>"1964 Nonaka Kofun Excavation Film Found," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' December 24, 2012.</ref>

{{Infobox Kofun | name = Nonaka Kofun | native_name = 野中古墳 | location = {{ill|Fujiidera, Osaka|ja|藤井寺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|33|36.9|N|135|36|9.3|E|display=inline,title}} | region = Japan | type = {{ill|Square tumulus (Hōfun)|ja|方墳}} | length = 37m per side | built = 5th century | burial = Wooden coffin, wooden chest | artifacts = Armor, iron weapons | designated = National [[Historic Site of Japan|Historic Site]] (part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}) | map = Japan Osaka | map_caption = Location in Osaka Prefecture | image = {{Image requested|cat=Fujiidera, Osaka}} }} '''Nonaka Kofun''' (野中古墳) is a mid-[[Kofun period]] burial mound ([[kofun]]) located in {{ill|Fujidera|ja|藤井寺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. A significant number of iron weapons, armor, and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} have been excavated from the site. The kofun is designated as a part of the [[UNESCO]] [[World Cultural Heritage]] "Ancient Tumulus Clusters," specifically within the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}}. It is also a [[National Historic Site of Japan]].
== Overview == The Nonaka Kofun is part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} and is situated north of the rear circle of the {{ill|Makasuyama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳}}, leading to speculation that it may have served as a {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|subsidiary burial mound}} for the latter.<ref name="yomiuri140212">"Restoration Completed for Nonaka Kofun Armor," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' February 12, 2014.</ref>
The mound is a square-shaped tumulus (方墳) built in two tiers, with each side measuring 37 meters and a height of 4 meters. It features a surrounding moat, {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (roofing stones), cylindrical haniwa rows, and figurative haniwa, including helmets, "Kinugasa" lids, and horned helmets (衝角付冑). Numerous {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} were excavated from the mound's summit.<ref name="yomiuri140212"/>
Beneath the mound's summit, five sets of burial facilities were aligned in parallel, consisting of wooden coffins and wooden chests. Only the second wooden coffin is believed to have contained human remains, while the other four are interpreted as dedicated for grave goods. These facilities yielded a significant collection of grave goods, including: * 11 sets of iron armor ([[tankō]] 短甲) * 11 helmets (冑) * Components such as grass skirts and shoulder guards * Iron swords (鉄刀), iron spears (鉄剣), and a large number of iron arrowheads (鉄鏃).
The abundant iron artifacts, Sue ware, and weapons provide invaluable insights into the burial practices of the mid-Kofun period. The tumulus is estimated to have been built during the early to mid-5th century.
== Excavations and Preservation == In 1964, an excavation led by [[Osaka University]] unearthed approximately 2,000 artifacts.<ref>"Crowdfunding Advances for 3D Digitalization of Important Artifacts," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' December 22, 2018.</ref> The site was designated as a National Historic Site in 1995 and incorporated into the unified designation of the "Furuichi Kofun Cluster" in 2001. The iron armor and other artifacts are stored at Osaka University and were designated as Important Cultural Properties of Japan in 2015.<ref>"Osaka City Art Museum Registered as Cultural Property," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' March 15, 2015.</ref>
Additionally, a 1964 color film documenting the excavation was rediscovered in 2009.<ref>"1964 Nonaka Kofun Excavation Film Found," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' December 24, 2012.</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Literature == * ''Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia,'' Tokyo-dō Shuppan, 1989.
== Related Sites == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} * {{ill|Nonaka Ariyama Kofun|ja|野中アリ山古墳}} * {{ill|Katombo-yama Kofun|ja|カトンボ山古墳}} - A baizuka featuring similar abundant burial goods, including imitation stone products and beads.
== External Links == * [http://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/10kakuka/34shakaikyoiku/03bunkazai/04isekishokai/13kaisetsu/k_nonaka.html Nonaka Kofun] (Habikino City Website) * [http://www.let.osaka-u.ac.jp/kouko/nonaka/ Nonaka Kofun] (Osaka University Archaeology Laboratory)

{{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Fujidera]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Nonaka Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Nonaka Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Nonaka Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[Category:kk]]{{Kofun navbar}}[[Category:kk]] [[ja:野中古墳]]

{{Infobox Kofun | name = Nonaka Kofun | native_name = 野中古墳 | location = {{ill|Fujiidera, Osaka|ja|藤井寺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|33|36.9|N|135|36|9.3|E|display=inline,title}} | region = Japan | type = {{ill|Square tumulus (Hōfun)|ja|方墳}} | length = 37m per side | built = 5th century | burial = Wooden coffin, wooden chest | artifacts = Armor, iron weapons | designated = National [[Historic Site of Japan|Historic Site]] (part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}) | map = Japan Osaka | map_caption = Location in Osaka Prefecture | image = {{Image requested|cat=Fujiidera, Osaka}} }} '''Nonaka Kofun''' (野中古墳) is a mid-[[Kofun period]] burial mound ([[kofun]]) located in {{ill|Fujidera|ja|藤井寺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. A significant number of iron weapons, armor, and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} have been excavated from the site. The kofun is designated as a part of the [[UNESCO]] [[World Cultural Heritage]] "Ancient Tumulus Clusters," specifically within the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}}. It is also a [[National Historic Site of Japan]].
== Overview == The Nonaka Kofun is part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} and is situated north of the rear circle of the {{ill|Makasuyama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳}}, leading to speculation that it may have served as a {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|subsidiary burial mound}} for the latter.<ref name="yomiuri140212">"Restoration Completed for Nonaka Kofun Armor," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' February 12, 2014.</ref>
The mound is a square-shaped tumulus (方墳) built in two tiers, with each side measuring 37 meters and a height of 4 meters. It features a surrounding moat, {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (roofing stones), cylindrical haniwa rows, and figurative haniwa, including helmets, "Kinugasa" lids, and horned helmets (衝角付冑). Numerous {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} were excavated from the mound's summit.<ref name="yomiuri140212"/>
Beneath the mound's summit, five sets of burial facilities were aligned in parallel, consisting of wooden coffins and wooden chests. Only the second wooden coffin is believed to have contained human remains, while the other four are interpreted as dedicated for grave goods. These facilities yielded a significant collection of grave goods, including: * 11 sets of iron armor ([[tankō]] 短甲) * 11 helmets (冑) * Components such as grass skirts and shoulder guards * Iron swords (鉄刀), iron spears (鉄剣), and a large number of iron arrowheads (鉄鏃).
The abundant iron artifacts, Sue ware, and weapons provide invaluable insights into the burial practices of the mid-Kofun period. The tumulus is estimated to have been built during the early to mid-5th century.
== Excavations and Preservation == In 1964, an excavation led by [[Osaka University]] unearthed approximately 2,000 artifacts.<ref>"Crowdfunding Advances for 3D Digitalization of Important Artifacts," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' December 22, 2018.</ref> The site was designated as a National Historic Site in 1995 and incorporated into the unified designation of the "Furuichi Kofun Cluster" in 2001. The iron armor and other artifacts are stored at Osaka University and were designated as Important Cultural Properties of Japan in 2015.<ref>"Osaka City Art Museum Registered as Cultural Property," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' March 15, 2015.</ref>
Additionally, a 1964 color film documenting the excavation was rediscovered in 2009.<ref>"1964 Nonaka Kofun Excavation Film Found," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' December 24, 2012.</ref>

revision.text:
{{Infobox Kofun | name = Nonaka Kofun | native_name = 野中古墳 | location = {{ill|Fujiidera, Osaka|ja|藤井寺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|33|36.9|N|135|36|9.3|E|display=inline,title}} | region = Japan | type = {{ill|Square tumulus (Hōfun)|ja|方墳}} | length = 37m per side | built = 5th century | burial = Wooden coffin, wooden chest | artifacts = Armor, iron weapons | designated = National [[Historic Site of Japan|Historic Site]] (part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}) | map = Japan Osaka | map_caption = Location in Osaka Prefecture | image = {{Image requested|cat=Fujiidera, Osaka}} }} '''Nonaka Kofun''' (野中古墳) is a mid-[[Kofun period]] burial mound ([[kofun]]) located in {{ill|Fujidera|ja|藤井寺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. A significant number of iron weapons, armor, and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} have been excavated from the site. The kofun is designated as a part of the [[UNESCO]] [[World Cultural Heritage]] "Ancient Tumulus Clusters," specifically within the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}}. It is also a [[National Historic Site of Japan]].
== Overview == The Nonaka Kofun is part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} and is situated north of the rear circle of the {{ill|Makasuyama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳}}, leading to speculation that it may have served as a {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|subsidiary burial mound}} for the latter.<ref name="yomiuri140212">"Restoration Completed for Nonaka Kofun Armor," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' February 12, 2014.</ref>
The mound is a square-shaped tumulus (方墳) built in two tiers, with each side measuring 37 meters and a height of 4 meters. It features a surrounding moat, {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (roofing stones), cylindrical haniwa rows, and figurative haniwa, including helmets, "Kinugasa" lids, and horned helmets (衝角付冑). Numerous {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} were excavated from the mound's summit.<ref name="yomiuri140212"/>
Beneath the mound's summit, five sets of burial facilities were aligned in parallel, consisting of wooden coffins and wooden chests. Only the second wooden coffin is believed to have contained human remains, while the other four are interpreted as dedicated for grave goods. These facilities yielded a significant collection of grave goods, including: * 11 sets of iron armor ([[tankō]] 短甲) * 11 helmets (冑) * Components such as grass skirts and shoulder guards * Iron swords (鉄刀), iron spears (鉄剣), and a large number of iron arrowheads (鉄鏃).
The abundant iron artifacts, Sue ware, and weapons provide invaluable insights into the burial practices of the mid-Kofun period. The tumulus is estimated to have been built during the early to mid-5th century.
== Excavations and Preservation == In 1964, an excavation led by [[Osaka University]] unearthed approximately 2,000 artifacts.<ref>"Crowdfunding Advances for 3D Digitalization of Important Artifacts," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' December 22, 2018.</ref> The site was designated as a National Historic Site in 1995 and incorporated into the unified designation of the "Furuichi Kofun Cluster" in 2001. The iron armor and other artifacts are stored at Osaka University and were designated as Important Cultural Properties of Japan in 2015.<ref>"Osaka City Art Museum Registered as Cultural Property," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' March 15, 2015.</ref>
Additionally, a 1964 color film documenting the excavation was rediscovered in 2009.<ref>"1964 Nonaka Kofun Excavation Film Found," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' December 24, 2012.</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Literature == * ''Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia,'' Tokyo-dō Shuppan, 1989.
== Related Sites == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} * {{ill|Nonaka Ariyama Kofun|ja|野中アリ山古墳}} * {{ill|Katombo-yama Kofun|ja|カトンボ山古墳}} - A baizuka featuring similar abundant burial goods, including imitation stone products and beads.
== External Links == * [http://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/10kakuka/34shakaikyoiku/03bunkazai/04isekishokai/13kaisetsu/k_nonaka.html Nonaka Kofun] (Habikino City Website) * [http://www.let.osaka-u.ac.jp/kouko/nonaka/ Nonaka Kofun] (Osaka University Archaeology Laboratory)

revision.text:
{{Infobox Kofun | name = Nonaka Kofun | native_name = 野中古墳 | location = {{ill|Fujiidera, Osaka|ja|藤井寺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|33|36.9|N|135|36|9.3|E|display=inline,title}} | region = Japan | type = {{ill|Square tumulus (Hōfun)|ja|方墳}} | length = 37m per side | built = 5th century | burial = Wooden coffin, wooden chest | artifacts = Armor, iron weapons | designated = National [[Historic Site of Japan|Historic Site]] (part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}) | map = Japan Osaka | map_caption = Location in Osaka Prefecture | image = {{Image requested|cat=Fujiidera, Osaka}} }} '''Nonaka Kofun''' (野中古墳) is a mid-[[Kofun period]] burial mound ([[kofun]]) located in {{ill|Fujidera|ja|藤井寺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. A significant number of iron weapons, armor, and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} have been excavated from the site. The kofun is designated as a part of the [[UNESCO]] [[World Cultural Heritage]] "Ancient Tumulus Clusters," specifically within the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}}. It is also a [[National Historic Site of Japan]].
== Overview == The Nonaka Kofun is part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} and is situated north of the rear circle of the {{ill|Makasuyama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳}}, leading to speculation that it may have served as a {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|subsidiary burial mound}} for the latter.<ref name="yomiuri140212">"Restoration Completed for Nonaka Kofun Armor," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' February 12, 2014.</ref>
The mound is a square-shaped tumulus (方墳) built in two tiers, with each side measuring 37 meters and a height of 4 meters. It features a surrounding moat, {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (roofing stones), cylindrical haniwa rows, and figurative haniwa, including helmets, "Kinugasa" lids, and horned helmets (衝角付冑). Numerous {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} were excavated from the mound's summit.<ref name="yomiuri140212"/>
Beneath the mound's summit, five sets of burial facilities were aligned in parallel, consisting of wooden coffins and wooden chests. Only the second wooden coffin is believed to have contained human remains, while the other four are interpreted as dedicated for grave goods. These facilities yielded a significant collection of grave goods, including: * 11 sets of iron armor ([[tankō]] 短甲) * 11 helmets (冑) * Components such as grass skirts and shoulder guards * Iron swords (鉄刀), iron spears (鉄剣), and a large number of iron arrowheads (鉄鏃).
The abundant iron artifacts, Sue ware, and weapons provide invaluable insights into the burial practices of the mid-Kofun period. The tumulus is estimated to have been built during the early to mid-5th century.
== Excavations and Preservation == In 1964, an excavation led by [[Osaka University]] unearthed approximately 2,000 artifacts.<ref>"Crowdfunding Advances for 3D Digitalization of Important Artifacts," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' December 22, 2018.</ref> The site was designated as a National Historic Site in 1995 and incorporated into the unified designation of the "Furuichi Kofun Cluster" in 2001. The iron armor and other artifacts are stored at Osaka University and were designated as Important Cultural Properties of Japan in 2015.<ref>"Osaka City Art Museum Registered as Cultural Property," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' March 15, 2015.</ref>
Additionally, a 1964 color film documenting the excavation was rediscovered in 2009.<ref>"1964 Nonaka Kofun Excavation Film Found," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' December 24, 2012.</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Literature == * ''Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia,'' Tokyo-dō Shuppan, 1989.
== Related Sites == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} * {{ill|Nonaka Ariyama Kofun|ja|野中アリ山古墳}} * {{ill|Katombo-yama Kofun|ja|カトンボ山古墳}} - A baizuka featuring similar abundant burial goods, including imitation stone products and beads.
== External Links == * [http://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/10kakuka/34shakaikyoiku/03bunkazai/04isekishokai/13kaisetsu/k_nonaka.html Nonaka Kofun] (Habikino City Website) * [http://www.let.osaka-u.ac.jp/kouko/nonaka/ Nonaka Kofun] (Osaka University Archaeology Laboratory)

The iron armor and other artifacts are stored at Osaka University and were designated as Important Cultural Properties of Japan in 2015.<ref>"Osaka City Art Museum Registered as Cultural Property," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' March 15, 2015.</ref>

{{Infobox Kofun | name = Nonaka Kofun | native_name = 野中古墳 | location = {{ill|Fujiidera, Osaka|ja|藤井寺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|33|36.9|N|135|36|9.3|E|display=inline,title}} | region = Japan | type = {{ill|Square tumulus (Hōfun)|ja|方墳}} | length = 37m per side | built = 5th century | burial = Wooden coffin, wooden chest | artifacts = Armor, iron weapons | designated = National [[Historic Site of Japan|Historic Site]] (part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}) | map = Japan Osaka | map_caption = Location in Osaka Prefecture | image = {{Image requested|cat=Fujiidera, Osaka}} }} '''Nonaka Kofun''' (野中古墳) is a mid-[[Kofun period]] burial mound ([[kofun]]) located in {{ill|Fujidera|ja|藤井寺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. A significant number of iron weapons, armor, and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} have been excavated from the site. The kofun is designated as a part of the [[UNESCO]] [[World Cultural Heritage]] "Ancient Tumulus Clusters," specifically within the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}}. It is also a [[National Historic Site of Japan]].
== Overview == The Nonaka Kofun is part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} and is situated north of the rear circle of the {{ill|Makasuyama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳}}, leading to speculation that it may have served as a {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|subsidiary burial mound}} for the latter.<ref name="yomiuri140212">"Restoration Completed for Nonaka Kofun Armor," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' February 12, 2014.</ref>
The mound is a square-shaped tumulus (方墳) built in two tiers, with each side measuring 37 meters and a height of 4 meters. It features a surrounding moat, {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (roofing stones), cylindrical haniwa rows, and figurative haniwa, including helmets, "Kinugasa" lids, and horned helmets (衝角付冑). Numerous {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} were excavated from the mound's summit.<ref name="yomiuri140212"/>
Beneath the mound's summit, five sets of burial facilities were aligned in parallel, consisting of wooden coffins and wooden chests. Only the second wooden coffin is believed to have contained human remains, while the other four are interpreted as dedicated for grave goods. These facilities yielded a significant collection of grave goods, including: * 11 sets of iron armor ([[tankō]] 短甲) * 11 helmets (冑) * Components such as grass skirts and shoulder guards * Iron swords (鉄刀), iron spears (鉄剣), and a large number of iron arrowheads (鉄鏃).
The abundant iron artifacts, Sue ware, and weapons provide invaluable insights into the burial practices of the mid-Kofun period. The tumulus is estimated to have been built during the early to mid-5th century.
== Excavations and Preservation == In 1964, an excavation led by [[Osaka University]] unearthed approximately 2,000 artifacts.<ref>"Crowdfunding Advances for 3D Digitalization of Important Artifacts," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' December 22, 2018.</ref> The site was designated as a National Historic Site in 1995 and incorporated into the unified designation of the "Furuichi Kofun Cluster" in 2001. The iron armor and other artifacts are stored at Osaka University and were designated as Important Cultural Properties of Japan in 2015.<ref>"Osaka City Art Museum Registered as Cultural Property," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' March 15, 2015.</ref>
Additionally, a 1964 color film documenting the excavation was rediscovered in 2009.<ref>"1964 Nonaka Kofun Excavation Film Found," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' December 24, 2012.</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Literature == * ''Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia,'' Tokyo-dō Shuppan, 1989.
== Related Sites == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} * {{ill|Nonaka Ariyama Kofun|ja|野中アリ山古墳}} * {{ill|Katombo-yama Kofun|ja|カトンボ山古墳}} - A baizuka featuring similar abundant burial goods, including imitation stone products and beads.
== External Links == * [http://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/10kakuka/34shakaikyoiku/03bunkazai/04isekishokai/13kaisetsu/k_nonaka.html Nonaka Kofun] (Habikino City Website) * [http://www.let.osaka-u.ac.jp/kouko/nonaka/ Nonaka Kofun] (Osaka University Archaeology Laboratory)

{{Infobox Kofun | name = Nonaka Kofun | native_name = 野中古墳 | location = {{ill|Fujiidera, Osaka|ja|藤井寺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|33|36.9|N|135|36|9.3|E|display=inline,title}} | region = Japan | type = {{ill|Square tumulus (Hōfun)|ja|方墳}} | length = 37m per side | built = 5th century | burial = Wooden coffin, wooden chest | artifacts = Armor, iron weapons | designated = National [[Historic Site of Japan|Historic Site]] (part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}) | map = Japan Osaka | map_caption = Location in Osaka Prefecture | image = {{Image requested|cat=Fujiidera, Osaka}} }} '''Nonaka Kofun''' (野中古墳) is a mid-[[Kofun period]] burial mound ([[kofun]]) located in {{ill|Fujidera|ja|藤井寺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. A significant number of iron weapons, armor, and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} have been excavated from the site. The kofun is designated as a part of the [[UNESCO]] [[World Cultural Heritage]] "Ancient Tumulus Clusters," specifically within the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}}. It is also a [[National Historic Site of Japan]].
== Overview == The Nonaka Kofun is part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} and is situated north of the rear circle of the {{ill|Makasuyama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳}}, leading to speculation that it may have served as a {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|subsidiary burial mound}} for the latter.<ref name="yomiuri140212">"Restoration Completed for Nonaka Kofun Armor," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' February 12, 2014.</ref>
The mound is a square-shaped tumulus (方墳) built in two tiers, with each side measuring 37 meters and a height of 4 meters. It features a surrounding moat, {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (roofing stones), cylindrical haniwa rows, and figurative haniwa, including helmets, "Kinugasa" lids, and horned helmets (衝角付冑). Numerous {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} were excavated from the mound's summit.<ref name="yomiuri140212"/>
Beneath the mound's summit, five sets of burial facilities were aligned in parallel, consisting of wooden coffins and wooden chests. Only the second wooden coffin is believed to have contained human remains, while the other four are interpreted as dedicated for grave goods. These facilities yielded a significant collection of grave goods, including: * 11 sets of iron armor ([[tankō]] 短甲) * 11 helmets (冑) * Components such as grass skirts and shoulder guards * Iron swords (鉄刀), iron spears (鉄剣), and a large number of iron arrowheads (鉄鏃).
The abundant iron artifacts, Sue ware, and weapons provide invaluable insights into the burial practices of the mid-Kofun period. The tumulus is estimated to have been built during the early to mid-5th century.
== Excavations and Preservation == In 1964, an excavation led by [[Osaka University]] unearthed approximately 2,000 artifacts.<ref>"Crowdfunding Advances for 3D Digitalization of Important Artifacts," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' December 22, 2018.</ref> The site was designated as a National Historic Site in 1995 and incorporated into the unified designation of the "Furuichi Kofun Cluster" in 2001. The iron armor and other artifacts are stored at Osaka University and were designated as Important Cultural Properties of Japan in 2015.<ref>"Osaka City Art Museum Registered as Cultural Property," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' March 15, 2015.</ref>
Additionally, a 1964 color film documenting the excavation was rediscovered in 2009.<ref>"1964 Nonaka Kofun Excavation Film Found," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' December 24, 2012.</ref>

{{Infobox Kofun | name = Nonaka Kofun | native_name = 野中古墳 | location = {{ill|Fujiidera, Osaka|ja|藤井寺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|33|36.9|N|135|36|9.3|E|display=inline,title}} | region = Japan | type = {{ill|Square tumulus (Hōfun)|ja|方墳}} | length = 37m per side | built = 5th century | burial = Wooden coffin, wooden chest | artifacts = Armor, iron weapons | designated = National [[Historic Site of Japan|Historic Site]] (part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}) | map = Japan Osaka | map_caption = Location in Osaka Prefecture | image = {{image requested|cat=Fujiidera, Osaka}} }} '''Nonaka Kofun''' (野中古墳) is a mid-[[Kofun period]] burial mound ([[kofun]]) located in {{ill|Fujidera|ja|藤井寺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. A significant number of iron weapons, armor, and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} have been excavated from the site. The kofun is designated as a part of the [[UNESCO]] [[World Cultural Heritage]] "Ancient Tumulus Clusters," specifically within the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}}. It is also a [[National Historic Site of Japan]].
== Overview == The Nonaka Kofun is part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} and is situated north of the rear circle of the {{ill|Makasuyama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳}}, leading to speculation that it may have served as a {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|subsidiary burial mound}} for the latter.<ref name="yomiuri140212">"Restoration Completed for Nonaka Kofun Armor," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' February 12, 2014.</ref>
The mound is a square-shaped tumulus (方墳) built in two tiers, with each side measuring 37 meters and a height of 4 meters. It features a surrounding moat, {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (roofing stones), cylindrical haniwa rows, and figurative haniwa, including helmets, "Kinugasa" lids, and horned helmets (衝角付冑). Numerous {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} were excavated from the mound's summit.<ref name="yomiuri140212"/>
Beneath the mound's summit, five sets of burial facilities were aligned in parallel, consisting of wooden coffins and wooden chests. Only the second wooden coffin is believed to have contained human remains, while the other four are interpreted as dedicated for grave goods. These facilities yielded a significant collection of grave goods, including: * 11 sets of iron armor ([[tankō]] 短甲) * 11 helmets (冑) * Components such as grass skirts and shoulder guards * Iron swords (鉄刀), iron spears (鉄剣), and a large number of iron arrowheads (鉄鏃).
The abundant iron artifacts, Sue ware, and weapons provide invaluable insights into the burial practices of the mid-Kofun period. The tumulus is estimated to have been built during the early to mid-5th century.
== Excavations and Preservation == In 1964, an excavation led by [[Osaka University]] unearthed approximately 2,000 artifacts.<ref>"Crowdfunding Advances for 3D Digitalization of Important Artifacts," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' December 22, 2018.</ref> The site was designated as a National Historic Site in 1995 and incorporated into the unified designation of the "Furuichi Kofun Cluster" in 2001. The iron armor and other artifacts are stored at Osaka University and were designated as Important Cultural Properties of Japan in 2015.<ref>"Osaka City Art Museum Registered as Cultural Property," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' March 15, 2015.</ref>
Additionally, a 1964 color film documenting the excavation was rediscovered in 2009.<ref>"1964 Nonaka Kofun Excavation Film Found," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' December 24, 2012.</ref>

{{Infobox Kofun | name = Nonaka Kofun | native_name = 野中古墳 | location = {{ill|Fujiidera, Osaka|ja|藤井寺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|33|36.9|N|135|36|9.3|E|display=inline,title}} | region = Japan | type = {{ill|Square tumulus (Hōfun)|ja|方墳}} | length = 37m per side | built = 5th century | burial = Wooden coffin, wooden chest | artifacts = Armor, iron weapons | designated = National [[Historic Site of Japan|Historic Site]] (part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}) | map = Japan Osaka | map_caption = Location in Osaka Prefecture | image = {{image requested|cat=Fujiidera, Osaka}} }} '''Nonaka Kofun''' (野中古墳) is a mid-[[Kofun period]] burial mound ([[kofun]]) located in {{ill|Fujidera|ja|藤井寺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. A significant number of iron weapons, armor, and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} have been excavated from the site. The kofun is designated as a part of the [[UNESCO]] [[World Cultural Heritage]] "Ancient Tumulus Clusters," specifically within the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}}. It is also a [[National Historic Site of Japan]].
== Overview == The Nonaka Kofun is part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} and is situated north of the rear circle of the {{ill|Makasuyama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳}}, leading to speculation that it may have served as a {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|subsidiary burial mound}} for the latter.<ref name="yomiuri140212">"Restoration Completed for Nonaka Kofun Armor," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' February 12, 2014.</ref>
The mound is a square-shaped tumulus (方墳) built in two tiers, with each side measuring 37 meters and a height of 4 meters. It features a surrounding moat, {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (roofing stones), cylindrical haniwa rows, and figurative haniwa, including helmets, "Kinugasa" lids, and horned helmets (衝角付冑). Numerous {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} were excavated from the mound's summit.<ref name="yomiuri140212"/>
Beneath the mound's summit, five sets of burial facilities were aligned in parallel, consisting of wooden coffins and wooden chests. Only the second wooden coffin is believed to have contained human remains, while the other four are interpreted as dedicated for grave goods. These facilities yielded a significant collection of grave goods, including: * 11 sets of iron armor ([[tankō]] 短甲) * 11 helmets (冑) * Components such as grass skirts and shoulder guards * Iron swords (鉄刀), iron spears (鉄剣), and a large number of iron arrowheads (鉄鏃).
The abundant iron artifacts, Sue ware, and weapons provide invaluable insights into the burial practices of the mid-Kofun period. The tumulus is estimated to have been built during the early to mid-5th century.
== Excavations and Preservation == In 1964, an excavation led by [[Osaka University]] unearthed approximately 2,000 artifacts.<ref>"Crowdfunding Advances for 3D Digitalization of Important Artifacts," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' December 22, 2018.</ref> The site was designated as a National Historic Site in 1995 and incorporated into the unified designation of the "Furuichi Kofun Cluster" in 2001. The iron armor and other artifacts are stored at Osaka University and were designated as Important Cultural Properties of Japan in 2015.<ref>"Osaka City Art Museum Registered as Cultural Property," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' March 15, 2015.</ref>
Additionally, a 1964 color film documenting the excavation was rediscovered in 2009.<ref>"1964 Nonaka Kofun Excavation Film Found," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' December 24, 2012.</ref>

These facilities yielded a significant collection of grave goods, including: * 11 sets of iron armor ([[tankō]] 短甲) * 11 helmets (冑) * Components such as grass skirts and shoulder guards * Iron swords (鉄刀), iron spears (鉄剣), and a large number of iron arrowheads (鉄鏃).
The abundant iron artifacts, Sue ware, and weapons provide invaluable insights into the burial practices of the mid-Kofun period. The tumulus is estimated to have been built during the early to mid-5th century.
== Excavations and Preservation == In 1964, an excavation led by [[Osaka University]] unearthed approximately 2,000 artifacts.<ref>"Crowdfunding Advances for 3D Digitalization of Important Artifacts," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' December 22, 2018.</ref> The site was designated as a National Historic Site in 1995 and incorporated into the unified designation of the "Furuichi Kofun Cluster" in 2001. The iron armor and other artifacts are stored at Osaka University and were designated as Important Cultural Properties of Japan in 2015.<ref>"Osaka City Art Museum Registered as Cultural Property," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' March 15, 2015.</ref>

{{Infobox Kofun | name = Nonaka Kofun | native_name = 野中古墳 | location = {{ill|Fujiidera, Osaka|ja|藤井寺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|33|36.9|N|135|36|9.3|E|display=inline,title}} | region = Japan | type = {{ill|Square tumulus (Hōfun)|ja|方墳}} | length = 37m per side | built = 5th century | burial = Wooden coffin, wooden chest | artifacts = Armor, iron weapons | designated = National [[Historic Site of Japan|Historic Site]] (part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}) | map = Japan Osaka | map_caption = Location in Osaka Prefecture | image = {{image requested|cat=Fujiidera, Osaka}} }} '''Nonaka Kofun''' (野中古墳) is a mid-[[Kofun period]] burial mound ([[kofun]]) located in {{ill|Fujidera|ja|藤井寺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. A significant number of iron weapons, armor, and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} have been excavated from the site. The kofun is designated as a part of the [[UNESCO]] [[World Cultural Heritage]] "Ancient Tumulus Clusters," specifically within the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}}. It is also a [[National Historic Site of Japan]].
== Overview == The Nonaka Kofun is part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} and is situated north of the rear circle of the {{ill|Makasuyama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳}}, leading to speculation that it may have served as a {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|subsidiary burial mound}} for the latter.<ref name="yomiuri140212">"Restoration Completed for Nonaka Kofun Armor," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' February 12, 2014.</ref>
The mound is a square-shaped tumulus (方墳) built in two tiers, with each side measuring 37 meters and a height of 4 meters. It features a surrounding moat, {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (roofing stones), cylindrical haniwa rows, and figurative haniwa, including helmets, "Kinugasa" lids, and horned helmets (衝角付冑). Numerous {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} were excavated from the mound's summit.<ref name="yomiuri140212"/>
Beneath the mound's summit, five sets of burial facilities were aligned in parallel, consisting of wooden coffins and wooden chests. Only the second wooden coffin is believed to have contained human remains, while the other four are interpreted as dedicated for grave goods. These facilities yielded a significant collection of grave goods, including: * 11 sets of iron armor ([[tankō]] 短甲) * 11 helmets (冑) * Components such as grass skirts and shoulder guards * Iron swords (鉄刀), iron spears (鉄剣), and a large number of iron arrowheads (鉄鏃).
The abundant iron artifacts, Sue ware, and weapons provide invaluable insights into the burial practices of the mid-Kofun period. The tumulus is estimated to have been built during the early to mid-5th century.
== Excavations and Preservation == In 1964, an excavation led by [[Osaka University]] unearthed approximately 2,000 artifacts.<ref>"Crowdfunding Advances for 3D Digitalization of Important Artifacts," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' December 22, 2018.</ref> The site was designated as a National Historic Site in 1995 and incorporated into the unified designation of the "Furuichi Kofun Cluster" in 2001. The iron armor and other artifacts are stored at Osaka University and were designated as Important Cultural Properties of Japan in 2015.<ref>"Osaka City Art Museum Registered as Cultural Property," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' March 15, 2015.</ref>
Additionally, a 1964 color film documenting the excavation was rediscovered in 2009.<ref>"1964 Nonaka Kofun Excavation Film Found," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' December 24, 2012.</ref>

The iron armor and other artifacts are stored at Osaka University and were designated as Important Cultural Properties of Japan in 2015.<ref>"Osaka City Art Museum Registered as Cultural Property," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' March 15, 2015.</ref>

{{Infobox Kofun | name = Nonaka Kofun | native_name = 野中古墳 | location = {{ill|Fujiidera, Osaka|ja|藤井寺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|33|36.9|N|135|36|9.3|E|display=inline,title}} | region = Japan | type = {{ill|Square tumulus (Hōfun)|ja|方墳}} | length = 37m per side | built = 5th century | burial = Wooden coffin, wooden chest | artifacts = Armor, iron weapons | designated = National [[Historic Site of Japan|Historic Site]] (part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}) | map = Japan Osaka | map_caption = Location in Osaka Prefecture | image = {{image requested|cat=Fujiidera, Osaka}} }} '''Nonaka Kofun''' (野中古墳) is a mid-[[Kofun period]] burial mound ([[kofun]]) located in {{ill|Fujidera|ja|藤井寺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. A significant number of iron weapons, armor, and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} have been excavated from the site. The kofun is designated as a part of the [[UNESCO]] [[World Cultural Heritage]] "Ancient Tumulus Clusters," specifically within the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}}. It is also a [[National Historic Site of Japan]].
== Overview == The Nonaka Kofun is part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} and is situated north of the rear circle of the {{ill|Makasuyama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳}}, leading to speculation that it may have served as a {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|subsidiary burial mound}} for the latter.<ref name="yomiuri140212">"Restoration Completed for Nonaka Kofun Armor," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' February 12, 2014.</ref>
The mound is a square-shaped tumulus (方墳) built in two tiers, with each side measuring 37 meters and a height of 4 meters. It features a surrounding moat, {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (roofing stones), cylindrical haniwa rows, and figurative haniwa, including helmets, "Kinugasa" lids, and horned helmets (衝角付冑). Numerous {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} were excavated from the mound's summit.<ref name="yomiuri140212"/>
Beneath the mound's summit, five sets of burial facilities were aligned in parallel, consisting of wooden coffins and wooden chests. Only the second wooden coffin is believed to have contained human remains, while the other four are interpreted as dedicated for grave goods. These facilities yielded a significant collection of grave goods, including: * 11 sets of iron armor ([[tankō]] 短甲) * 11 helmets (冑) * Components such as grass skirts and shoulder guards * Iron swords (鉄刀), iron spears (鉄剣), and a large number of iron arrowheads (鉄鏃).
The abundant iron artifacts, Sue ware, and weapons provide invaluable insights into the burial practices of the mid-Kofun period. The tumulus is estimated to have been built during the early to mid-5th century.
== Excavations and Preservation == In 1964, an excavation led by [[Osaka University]] unearthed approximately 2,000 artifacts.<ref>"Crowdfunding Advances for 3D Digitalization of Important Artifacts," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' December 22, 2018.</ref> The site was designated as a National Historic Site in 1995 and incorporated into the unified designation of the "Furuichi Kofun Cluster" in 2001. The iron armor and other artifacts are stored at Osaka University and were designated as Important Cultural Properties of Japan in 2015.<ref>"Osaka City Art Museum Registered as Cultural Property," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' March 15, 2015.</ref>
Additionally, a 1964 color film documenting the excavation was rediscovered in 2009.<ref>"1964 Nonaka Kofun Excavation Film Found," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' December 24, 2012.</ref>

[[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Nonaka Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:Nonaka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:suQqqqqq]] [[Category:suQqqqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[ja:野中古墳]] [[Category:new pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nonaka Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nonaka Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nonaka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:suQqqqqq]][[sn:Nonaka Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nonaka Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nonaka Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nonaka Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nonaka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nonaka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nonaka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:suQqqqqq]][[sn:Nonaka Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nonaka Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nonaka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nonaka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:方墳]][[sn:Nonaka Kofun]][[Category:方墳]] [[Category:方墳]][[sn:Nonaka Kofun]][[Category:方墳]] [[Category:方墳]][[sn:Nonaka Kofun]][[Category:方墳]] [[Category:方墳]][[sn:Nonaka Kofun]][[Category:方墳]] [[Category:方墳]][[sn:Nonaka Kofun]][[Category:方墳]]

{{Infobox Kofun | name = Nonaka Kofun | native_name = 野中古墳 | location = {{ill|Fujiidera, Osaka|ja|藤井寺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|33|36.9|N|135|36|9.3|E|display=inline,title}} | region = Japan | type = {{ill|Square tumulus (Hōfun)|ja|方墳}} | length = 37m per side | built = 5th century | burial = Wooden coffin, wooden chest | artifacts = Armor, iron weapons | designated = National [[Historic Site of Japan|Historic Site]] (part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}) | map = Japan Osaka | map_caption = Location in Osaka Prefecture | image = {{image requested|cat=Fujiidera, Osaka}} }} '''Nonaka Kofun''' (野中古墳) is a mid-[[Kofun period]] burial mound ([[kofun]]) located in {{ill|Fujidera|ja|藤井寺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. A significant number of iron weapons, armor, and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} have been excavated from the site. The kofun is designated as a part of the [[UNESCO]] [[World Cultural Heritage]] "Ancient Tumulus Clusters," specifically within the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}}. It is also a [[National Historic Site of Japan]].
== Overview == The Nonaka Kofun is part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} and is situated north of the rear circle of the {{ill|Makasuyama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳}}, leading to speculation that it may have served as a {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|subsidiary burial mound}} for the latter.<ref name="yomiuri140212">"Restoration Completed for Nonaka Kofun Armor," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' February 12, 2014.</ref>
The mound is a square-shaped tumulus (方墳) built in two tiers, with each side measuring 37 meters and a height of 4 meters. It features a surrounding moat, {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (roofing stones), cylindrical haniwa rows, and figurative haniwa, including helmets, "Kinugasa" lids, and horned helmets (衝角付冑). Numerous {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} were excavated from the mound's summit.<ref name="yomiuri140212"/>
Beneath the mound's summit, five sets of burial facilities were aligned in parallel, consisting of wooden coffins and wooden chests. Only the second wooden coffin is believed to have contained human remains, while the other four are interpreted as dedicated for grave goods. These facilities yielded a significant collection of grave goods, including: * 11 sets of iron armor ([[tankō]] 短甲) * 11 helmets (冑) * Components such as grass skirts and shoulder guards * Iron swords (鉄刀), iron spears (鉄剣), and a large number of iron arrowheads (鉄鏃).
The abundant iron artifacts, Sue ware, and weapons provide invaluable insights into the burial practices of the mid-Kofun period. The tumulus is estimated to have been built during the early to mid-5th century.
== Excavations and Preservation == In 1964, an excavation led by [[Osaka University]] unearthed approximately 2,000 artifacts.<ref>"Crowdfunding Advances for 3D Digitalization of Important Artifacts," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' December 22, 2018.</ref> The site was designated as a National Historic Site in 1995 and incorporated into the unified designation of the "Furuichi Kofun Cluster" in 2001. The iron armor and other artifacts are stored at Osaka University and were designated as Important Cultural Properties of Japan in 2015.<ref>"Osaka City Art Museum Registered as Cultural Property," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' March 15, 2015.</ref>
Additionally, a 1964 color film documenting the excavation was rediscovered in 2009.<ref>"1964 Nonaka Kofun Excavation Film Found," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' December 24, 2012.</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Literature == * ''Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia,'' Tokyo-dō Shuppan, 1989.
== Related Sites == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} * {{ill|Nonaka Ariyama Kofun|ja|野中アリ山古墳}} * {{ill|Katombo-yama Kofun|ja|カトンボ山古墳}} - A baizuka featuring similar abundant burial goods, including imitation stone products and beads.
== External Links == * [http://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/10kakuka/34shakaikyoiku/03bunkazai/04isekishokai/13kaisetsu/k_nonaka.html Nonaka Kofun] (Habikino City Website) * [http://www.let.osaka-u.ac.jp/kouko/nonaka/ Nonaka Kofun] (Osaka University Archaeology Laboratory)

[[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Nonaka Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:Nonaka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:suQqqqqq]] [[Category:suQqqqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[ja:野中古墳]] [[Category:new pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nonaka Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nonaka Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nonaka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:suQqqqqq]][[sn:Nonaka Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nonaka Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nonaka Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nonaka Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nonaka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nonaka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nonaka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:suQqqqqq]][[sn:Nonaka Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nonaka Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nonaka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nonaka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:方墳]][[sn:Nonaka Kofun]][[Category:方墳]] [[Category:方墳]][[sn:Nonaka Kofun]][[Category:方墳]] [[Category:方墳]][[sn:Nonaka Kofun]][[Category:方墳]] [[Category:方墳]][[sn:Nonaka Kofun]][[Category:方墳]] [[Category:方墳]][[sn:Nonaka Kofun]][[Category:方墳]] [[Category:方墳]][[sn:Nonaka Kofun]][[Category:方墳]] [[Category:方墳]][[sn:Nonaka Kofun]][[Category:方墳]] [[Category:方墳]][[sn:Nonaka Kofun]][[Category:方墳]]

{{Infobox Kofun | name = Nonaka Kofun | native_name = 野中古墳 | location = {{ill|Fujiidera, Osaka|ja|藤井寺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|33|36.9|N|135|36|9.3|E|display=inline,title}} | region = Japan | type = {{ill|Square tumulus (Hōfun)|ja|方墳}} | length = 37m per side | built = 5th century | burial = Wooden coffin, wooden chest | artifacts = Armor, iron weapons | designated = National [[Historic Site of Japan|Historic Site]] (part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}) | map = Japan Osaka | map_caption = Location in Osaka Prefecture | image = {{image requested|cat=Fujiidera, Osaka}} }} '''Nonaka Kofun''' (野中古墳) is a mid-[[Kofun period]] burial mound ([[kofun]]) located in {{ill|Fujidera|ja|藤井寺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. A significant number of iron weapons, armor, and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} have been excavated from the site. The kofun is designated as a part of the [[UNESCO]] [[World Cultural Heritage]] "Ancient Tumulus Clusters," specifically within the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}}. It is also a [[National Historic Site of Japan]].
== Overview == The Nonaka Kofun is part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} and is situated north of the rear circle of the {{ill|Makasuyama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳}}, leading to speculation that it may have served as a {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|subsidiary burial mound}} for the latter.<ref name="yomiuri140212">"Restoration Completed for Nonaka Kofun Armor," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' February 12, 2014.</ref>
The mound is a square-shaped tumulus (方墳) built in two tiers, with each side measuring 37 meters and a height of 4 meters. It features a surrounding moat, {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (roofing stones), cylindrical haniwa rows, and figurative haniwa, including helmets, "Kinugasa" lids, and horned helmets (衝角付冑). Numerous {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} were excavated from the mound's summit.<ref name="yomiuri140212"/>
Beneath the mound's summit, five sets of burial facilities were aligned in parallel, consisting of wooden coffins and wooden chests. Only the second wooden coffin is believed to have contained human remains, while the other four are interpreted as dedicated for grave goods. These facilities yielded a significant collection of grave goods, including: * 11 sets of iron armor ([[tankō]] 短甲) * 11 helmets (冑) * Components such as grass skirts and shoulder guards * Iron swords (鉄刀), iron spears (鉄剣), and a large number of iron arrowheads (鉄鏃).
The abundant iron artifacts, Sue ware, and weapons provide invaluable insights into the burial practices of the mid-Kofun period. The tumulus is estimated to have been built during the early to mid-5th century.
== Excavations and Preservation == In 1964, an excavation led by [[Osaka University]] unearthed approximately 2,000 artifacts.<ref>"Crowdfunding Advances for 3D Digitalization of Important Artifacts," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' December 22, 2018.</ref> The site was designated as a National Historic Site in 1995 and incorporated into the unified designation of the "Furuichi Kofun Cluster" in 2001. The iron armor and other artifacts are stored at Osaka University and were designated as Important Cultural Properties of Japan in 2015.<ref>"Osaka City Art Museum Registered as Cultural Property," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' March 15, 2015.</ref>
Additionally, a 1964 color film documenting the excavation was rediscovered in 2009.<ref>"1964 Nonaka Kofun Excavation Film Found," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' December 24, 2012.</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Literature == * ''Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia,'' Tokyo-dō Shuppan, 1989.
== Related Sites == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} * {{ill|Nonaka Ariyama Kofun|ja|野中アリ山古墳}} * {{ill|Katombo-yama Kofun|ja|カトンボ山古墳}} - A baizuka featuring similar abundant burial goods, including imitation stone products and beads.
== External Links == * [http://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/10kakuka/34shakaikyoiku/03bunkazai/04isekishokai/13kaisetsu/k_nonaka.html Nonaka Kofun] (Habikino City Website) * [http://www.let.osaka-u.ac.jp/kouko/nonaka/ Nonaka Kofun] (Osaka University Archaeology Laboratory)

{{Infobox Kofun | name = Nonaka Kofun | native_name = 野中古墳 | location = {{ill|Fujiidera, Osaka|ja|藤井寺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|33|36.9|N|135|36|9.3|E|display=inline,title}} | region = Japan | type = {{ill|Square tumulus (Hōfun)|ja|方墳}} | length = 37m per side | built = 5th century | burial = Wooden coffin, wooden chest | artifacts = Armor, iron weapons | designated = National [[Historic Site of Japan|Historic Site]] (part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}) | map = Japan Osaka | map_caption = Location in Osaka Prefecture | image = {{image requested|cat=Fujiidera, Osaka}} }} '''Nonaka Kofun''' (野中古墳) is a mid-[[Kofun period]] burial mound ([[kofun]]) located in {{ill|Fujidera|ja|藤井寺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. A significant number of iron weapons, armor, and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} have been excavated from the site. The kofun is designated as a part of the [[UNESCO]] [[World Cultural Heritage]] "Ancient Tumulus Clusters," specifically within the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}}. It is also a [[National Historic Site of Japan]].
== Overview == The Nonaka Kofun is part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} and is situated north of the rear circle of the {{ill|Makasuyama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳}}, leading to speculation that it may have served as a {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|subsidiary burial mound}} for the latter.<ref name="yomiuri140212">"Restoration Completed for Nonaka Kofun Armor," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' February 12, 2014.</ref>
The mound is a square-shaped tumulus (方墳) built in two tiers, with each side measuring 37 meters and a height of 4 meters. It features a surrounding moat, {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (roofing stones), cylindrical haniwa rows, and figurative haniwa, including helmets, "Kinugasa" lids, and horned helmets (衝角付冑). Numerous {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} were excavated from the mound's summit.<ref name="yomiuri140212"/>
Beneath the mound's summit, five sets of burial facilities were aligned in parallel, consisting of wooden coffins and wooden chests. Only the second wooden coffin is believed to have contained human remains, while the other four are interpreted as dedicated for grave goods. These facilities yielded a significant collection of grave goods, including: * 11 sets of iron armor ([[tankō]] 短甲) * 11 helmets (冑) * Components such as grass skirts and shoulder guards * Iron swords (鉄刀), iron spears (鉄剣), and a large number of iron arrowheads (鉄鏃).
The abundant iron artifacts, Sue ware, and weapons provide invaluable insights into the burial practices of the mid-Kofun period. The tumulus is estimated to have been built during the early to mid-5th century.
== Excavations and Preservation == In 1964, an excavation led by [[Osaka University]] unearthed approximately 2,000 artifacts.<ref>"Crowdfunding Advances for 3D Digitalization of Important Artifacts," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' December 22, 2018.</ref> The site was designated as a National Historic Site in 1995 and incorporated into the unified designation of the "Furuichi Kofun Cluster" in 2001. The iron armor and other artifacts are stored at Osaka University and were designated as Important Cultural Properties of Japan in 2015.<ref>"Osaka City Art Museum Registered as Cultural Property," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' March 15, 2015.</ref>
Additionally, a 1964 color film documenting the excavation was rediscovered in 2009.<ref>"1964 Nonaka Kofun Excavation Film Found," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' December 24, 2012.</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Literature == * ''Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia,'' Tokyo-dō Shuppan, 1989.

{{日本の古墳 |名称= 野中古墳 |画像=[[File:Nonakakohun.jpg|300px]] |所属=[[古市古墳群]] |所在地=大阪府藤井寺市野中 |位置={{Coord|34|33|36.9|N|135|36|9.3|E|region:JP-27}} |形状=方墳 |規模=一辺37m |築造年代=5世紀 |埋葬施設=木棺 木櫃 |被葬者= |出土品=甲冑 鉄製武器 |指定文化財=国の[[史跡]] |特記事項= }} '''野中古墳'''（のなかこふん）は、[[大阪府]][[藤井寺市]]野中3丁目に所在する[[古墳時代]]中期の古墳（[[方墳]]）である。鉄製の武器、武具、[[須恵器]]など大量の遺物が出土した。[[文化遺産 (世界遺産)|世界文化遺産]]の「[[古市古墳群]]」の一部として国の[[史跡]]に指定されている。
== 概要== [[古市古墳群]]に属し、[[墓山古墳]]の後円部北側に位置しており同古墳の[[陪塚]]とも推定されている<ref name="yomiuri140212">『読売新聞』2014年2月12日朝刊文化面19頁「野中古墳 甲冑修復終える」（読売新聞東京本社）</ref>。墳丘は2段に築成された方墳であり、一辺37m{{R|yomiuri140212}}、高さ4m、周濠があり、[[葺石]]、[[円筒埴輪]]列、蓋（きぬがさ）、[[衝角付冑]]、囲形などの[[形象埴輪]]が存在し、墳頂部からは大量の[[須恵器]]、[[土師器]]が出土している{{R|yomiuri140212}}。墳頂部地下には組合式[[木棺]]と木櫃からなる内部主体が5列に並列して埋納されており、人体埋葬が考えられるのは第2列の木棺のみで、他の4列はすべて、副葬品埋納施設と推定される。それらの施設からは[[短甲]]11領、[[冑]]11箇のほか、草摺、肩甲などの鉄製武具、[[直刀|鉄刀]]、[[剣|鉄剣]]、[[鉄鏃]]などの大量の武器が出土している。大量の鉄製品、須恵質土器、武器・武具の副葬形態など中期古墳の研究の上で欠くことのできない内容の古墳である。5世紀前半から中葉頃の築造と考えられている。
1964年、[[大阪大学]]が実施した発掘調査により遺物約2000点が出土した<ref>『読売新聞』2018年12月22日朝刊大阪2面32頁「重文遺物3D化 前進 阪大計測計画 ネット募金 目標150万円達成＝大阪」（読売新聞大阪本社）</ref>。1995年に国の史跡に指定される。2001年、既指定の他の古墳と統合指定され、国の史跡「古市古墳群」の一部となっている。[[甲冑]]などの出土品は大阪大学に所蔵されており、2015年に国の重要文化財に指定されている<ref>『読売新聞』2015年3月15日朝刊市内面33頁「大阪市立美術館 国登録文化財に 審議会答申＝大阪」（読売新聞大阪本社）</ref>。なお、1964年に本遺跡の発掘調査を撮影したカラー映像が2009年に発見された<ref>『読売新聞』2012年12月24日朝刊河内面23頁「「百舌鳥・古市」野中古墳 48年前の発掘 鮮明映像＝大阪」（読売新聞大阪本社）</ref>。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 文献 == * 『日本古墳大辞典』、株式会社東京堂出版、1989年。

== 外部リンク == *[http://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/10kakuka/34shakaikyoiku/03bunkazai/04isekishokai/13kaisetsu/k_nonaka.html 野中古墳（のなかこふん）]（羽曳野市ウェブサイト） *[http://www.let.osaka-u.ac.jp/kouko/nonaka/ 野中古墳]（大阪大学考古学研究室）
