{{Infobox Kofun | name = Nonaka Kofun | native_name = 野中古墳 | location = {{ill|Fujiidera, Osaka|ja|藤井寺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|33|36.9|N|135|36|9.3|E|display=inline,title}} | region = Japan | type = {{ill|Square tumulus (Hōfun)|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} | length = 37m per side | built = 5th century | burial = Wooden coffin, wooden chest | artifacts = Armor, iron weapons | designated = National [[Historic Site of Japan|Historic Site]] (part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}) | map = Japan Osaka | map_caption = Location in Osaka Prefecture | image = {{image requested|cat=Fujiidera, Osaka}} }} '''Nonaka Kofun''' (野中古墳) is a mid-[[Kofun period]] burial mound ([[kofun]]) located in {{ill|Fujidera|ja|藤井寺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. A significant number of iron weapons, armor, and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} have been excavated from the site. The kofun is designated as a part of the [[UNESCO]] [[World Cultural Heritage]] "Ancient Tumulus Clusters," specifically within the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}}. It is also a [[National Historic Site of Japan]].
== Overview == The Nonaka Kofun is part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} and is situated north of the rear circle of the {{ill|Makasuyama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳}}, leading to speculation that it may have served as a {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|subsidiary burial mound}} for the latter.<ref name="yomiuri140212">"Restoration Completed for Nonaka Kofun Armor," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' February 12, 2014.</ref>
The mound is a square-shaped tumulus (方墳) built in two tiers, with each side measuring 37 meters and a height of 4 meters. It features a surrounding moat, {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (roofing stones), cylindrical haniwa rows, and figurative haniwa, including helmets, "Kinugasa" lids, and horned helmets (衝角付冑). Numerous {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} were excavated from the mound's summit.<ref name="yomiuri140212"/>
Beneath the mound's summit, five sets of burial facilities were aligned in parallel, consisting of wooden coffins and wooden chests. Only the second wooden coffin is believed to have contained human remains, while the other four are interpreted as dedicated for grave goods. These facilities yielded a significant collection of grave goods, including: * 11 sets of iron armor ([[tankō]] 短甲) * 11 helmets (冑) * Components such as grass skirts and shoulder guards * Iron swords (鉄刀), iron spears (鉄剣), and a large number of iron arrowheads (鉄鏃).
The abundant iron artifacts, Sue ware, and weapons provide invaluable insights into the burial practices of the mid-Kofun period. The tumulus is estimated to have been built during the early to mid-5th century.
== Excavations and Preservation == In 1964, an excavation led by [[Osaka University]] unearthed approximately 2,000 artifacts.<ref>"Crowdfunding Advances for 3D Digitalization of Important Artifacts," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' December 22, 2018.</ref> The site was designated as a National Historic Site in 1995 and incorporated into the unified designation of the "Furuichi Kofun Cluster" in 2001. The iron armor and other artifacts are stored at Osaka University and were designated as Important Cultural Properties of Japan in 2015.<ref>"Osaka City Art Museum Registered as Cultural Property," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' March 15, 2015.</ref>
Additionally, a 1964 color film documenting the excavation was rediscovered in 2009.<ref>"1964 Nonaka Kofun Excavation Film Found," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' December 24, 2012.</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Literature == * ''Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia,'' Tokyo-dō Shuppan, 1989.
== Related Sites == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} * {{ill|Nonaka Ariyama Kofun|ja|野中アリ山古墳}} * {{ill|Katombo-yama Kofun|ja|カトンボ山古墳}} - A baizuka featuring similar abundant burial goods, including imitation stone products and beads.
== External Links == * [http://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/10kakuka/34shakaikyoiku/03bunkazai/04isekishokai/13kaisetsu/k_nonaka.html Nonaka Kofun] (Habikino City Website) * [http://www.let.osaka-u.ac.jp/kouko/nonaka/ Nonaka Kofun] (Osaka University Archaeology Laboratory)

{{Infobox Kofun | name = Nonaka Kofun | native_name = 野中古墳 | location = {{ill|Fujiidera, Osaka|ja|藤井寺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|33|36.9|N|135|36|9.3|E|display=inline,title}} | region = Japan | type = {{ill|Square tumulus (Hōfun)|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} | length = 37m per side | built = 5th century | burial = Wooden coffin, wooden chest | artifacts = Armor, iron weapons | designated = National [[Historic Site of Japan|Historic Site]] (part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}) | map = Japan Osaka | map_caption = Location in Osaka Prefecture | image = {{image requested|cat=Fujiidera, Osaka}} }} '''Nonaka Kofun''' (野中古墳) is a mid-[[Kofun period]] burial mound ([[kofun]]) located in {{ill|Fujidera|ja|藤井寺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. A significant number of iron weapons, armor, and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} have been excavated from the site. The kofun is designated as a part of the [[UNESCO]] [[World Cultural Heritage]] "Ancient Tumulus Clusters," specifically within the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}}. It is also a [[National Historic Site of Japan]].
== Overview == The Nonaka Kofun is part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} and is situated north of the rear circle of the {{ill|Makasuyama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳|1=Hakayama Kofun}}, leading to speculation that it may have served as a {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|subsidiary burial mound}} for the latter.<ref name="yomiuri140212">"Restoration Completed for Nonaka Kofun Armor," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' February 12, 2014.</ref>
The mound is a square-shaped tumulus (方墳) built in two tiers, with each side measuring 37 meters and a height of 4 meters. It features a surrounding moat, {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (roofing stones), cylindrical haniwa rows, and figurative haniwa, including helmets, "Kinugasa" lids, and horned helmets (衝角付冑). Numerous {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} were excavated from the mound's summit.<ref name="yomiuri140212"/>
Beneath the mound's summit, five sets of burial facilities were aligned in parallel, consisting of wooden coffins and wooden chests. Only the second wooden coffin is believed to have contained human remains, while the other four are interpreted as dedicated for grave goods. These facilities yielded a significant collection of grave goods, including: * 11 sets of iron armor ([[tankō]] 短甲) * 11 helmets (冑) * Components such as grass skirts and shoulder guards * Iron swords (鉄刀), iron spears (鉄剣), and a large number of iron arrowheads (鉄鏃).
The abundant iron artifacts, Sue ware, and weapons provide invaluable insights into the burial practices of the mid-Kofun period. The tumulus is estimated to have been built during the early to mid-5th century.
== Excavations and Preservation == In 1964, an excavation led by [[Osaka University]] unearthed approximately 2,000 artifacts.<ref>"Crowdfunding Advances for 3D Digitalization of Important Artifacts," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' December 22, 2018.</ref> The site was designated as a National Historic Site in 1995 and incorporated into the unified designation of the "Furuichi Kofun Cluster" in 2001. The iron armor and other artifacts are stored at Osaka University and were designated as Important Cultural Properties of Japan in 2015.<ref>"Osaka City Art Museum Registered as Cultural Property," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' March 15, 2015.</ref>
Additionally, a 1964 color film documenting the excavation was rediscovered in 2009.<ref>"1964 Nonaka Kofun Excavation Film Found," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' December 24, 2012.</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Literature == * ''Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia,'' Tokyo-dō Shuppan, 1989.
== Related Sites == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} * {{ill|Nonaka Ariyama Kofun|ja|野中アリ山古墳}} * {{ill|Katombo-yama Kofun|ja|カトンボ山古墳}} - A baizuka featuring similar abundant burial goods, including imitation stone products and beads.
== External Links == * [http://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/10kakuka/34shakaikyoiku/03bunkazai/04isekishokai/13kaisetsu/k_nonaka.html Nonaka Kofun] (Habikino City Website) * [http://www.let.osaka-u.ac.jp/kouko/nonaka/ Nonaka Kofun] (Osaka University Archaeology Laboratory)

{{Infobox Kofun | name = Nonaka Kofun | native_name = 野中古墳 | location = {{ill|Fujiidera, Osaka|ja|藤井寺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|33|36.9|N|135|36|9.3|E|display=inline,title}} | region = Japan | type = {{ill|Square tumulus (Hōfun)|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} | length = 37m per side | built = 5th century | burial = Wooden coffin, wooden chest | artifacts = Armor, iron weapons | designated = National [[Historic Site of Japan|Historic Site]] (part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}) | map = Japan Osaka | map_caption = Location in Osaka Prefecture | image = {{image requested|cat=Fujiidera, Osaka}} }} '''Nonaka Kofun''' (野中古墳) is a mid-[[Kofun period]] burial mound ([[kofun]]) located in {{ill|Fujidera|ja|藤井寺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. A significant number of iron weapons, armor, and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} have been excavated from the site. The kofun is designated as a part of the [[UNESCO]] [[World Cultural Heritage]] "Ancient Tumulus Clusters," specifically within the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}}. It is also a [[National Historic Site of Japan]].
== Overview == The Nonaka Kofun is part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} and is situated north of the rear circle of the {{ill|Makasuyama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳|1=Hakayama Kofun}}, leading to speculation that it may have served as a {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|subsidiary burial mound}} for the latter.<ref name="yomiuri140212">"Restoration Completed for Nonaka Kofun Armor," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' February 12, 2014.</ref>
The mound is a square-shaped tumulus (方墳) built in two tiers, with each side measuring 37 meters and a height of 4 meters. It features a surrounding moat, {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (roofing stones), cylindrical haniwa rows, and figurative haniwa, including helmets, "Kinugasa" lids, and horned helmets (衝角付冑). Numerous {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} were excavated from the mound's summit.<ref name="yomiuri140212"/>
Beneath the mound's summit, five sets of burial facilities were aligned in parallel, consisting of wooden coffins and wooden chests. Only the second wooden coffin is believed to have contained human remains, while the other four are interpreted as dedicated for grave goods. These facilities yielded a significant collection of grave goods, including: * 11 sets of iron armor ([[tankō]] 短甲) * 11 helmets (冑) * Components such as grass skirts and shoulder guards * Iron swords (鉄刀), iron spears (鉄剣), and a large number of iron arrowheads (鉄鏃).
The abundant iron artifacts, Sue ware, and weapons provide invaluable insights into the burial practices of the mid-Kofun period. The tumulus is estimated to have been built during the early to mid-5th century.
== Excavations and Preservation == In 1964, an excavation led by [[Osaka University]] unearthed approximately 2,000 artifacts.<ref>"Crowdfunding Advances for 3D Digitalization of Important Artifacts," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' December 22, 2018.</ref> The site was designated as a National Historic Site in 1995 and incorporated into the unified designation of the "Furuichi Kofun Cluster" in 2001. The iron armor and other artifacts are stored at Osaka University and were designated as Important Cultural Properties of Japan in 2015.<ref>"Osaka City Art Museum Registered as Cultural Property," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' March 15, 2015.</ref>
Additionally, a 1964 color film documenting the excavation was rediscovered in 2009.<ref>"1964 Nonaka Kofun Excavation Film Found," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' December 24, 2012.</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Literature == * ''Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia,'' Tokyo-dō Shuppan, 1989.
== Related Sites == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} * {{ill|Nonaka Ariyama Kofun|ja|野中アリ山古墳}} * {{ill|Katombo-yama Kofun|ja|カトンボ山古墳}} - A baizuka featuring similar abundant burial goods, including imitation stone products and beads.
== External Links == * [http://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/10kakuka/34shakaikyoiku/03bunkazai/04isekishokai/13kaisetsu/k_nonaka.html Nonaka Kofun] (Habikino City Website) * [http://www.let.osaka-u.ac.jp/kouko/nonaka/ Nonaka Kofun] (Osaka University Archaeology Laboratory)

revision.text:
{{Infobox Kofun | name = Nonaka Kofun | native_name = 野中古墳 | location = {{ill|Fujiidera, Osaka|ja|藤井寺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|33|36.9|N|135|36|9.3|E|display=inline,title}} | region = Japan | type = {{ill|Square tumulus (Hōfun)|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} | length = 37m per side | built = 5th century | burial = Wooden coffin, wooden chest | artifacts = Armor, iron weapons | designated = National [[Historic Site of Japan|Historic Site]] (part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}) | map = Japan Osaka | map_caption = Location in Osaka Prefecture | image = {{image requested|cat=Fujiidera, Osaka}} }} '''Nonaka Kofun''' (野中古墳) is a mid-[[Kofun period]] burial mound ([[kofun]]) located in {{ill|Fujidera|ja|藤井寺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. A significant number of iron weapons, armor, and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} have been excavated from the site. The kofun is designated as a part of the [[UNESCO]] [[World Cultural Heritage]] "Ancient Tumulus Clusters," specifically within the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}}. It is also a [[National Historic Site of Japan]].
== Overview == The Nonaka Kofun is part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} and is situated north of the rear circle of the {{ill|Makasuyama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳|1=Hakayama Kofun}}, leading to speculation that it may have served as a {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|subsidiary burial mound}} for the latter.<ref name="yomiuri140212">"Restoration Completed for Nonaka Kofun Armor," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' February 12, 2014.</ref>
The mound is a square-shaped tumulus (方墳) built in two tiers, with each side measuring 37 meters and a height of 4 meters. It features a surrounding moat, {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (roofing stones), cylindrical haniwa rows, and figurative haniwa, including helmets, "Kinugasa" lids, and horned helmets (衝角付冑). Numerous {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} were excavated from the mound's summit.<ref name="yomiuri140212"/>
Beneath the mound's summit, five sets of burial facilities were aligned in parallel, consisting of wooden coffins and wooden chests. Only the second wooden coffin is believed to have contained human remains, while the other four are interpreted as dedicated for grave goods. These facilities yielded a significant collection of grave goods, including: * 11 sets of iron armor ([[tankō]] 短甲) * 11 helmets (冑) * Components such as grass skirts and shoulder guards * Iron swords (鉄刀), iron spears (鉄剣), and a large number of iron arrowheads (鉄鏃).
The abundant iron artifacts, Sue ware, and weapons provide invaluable insights into the burial practices of the mid-Kofun period. The tumulus is estimated to have been built during the early to mid-5th century.
== Excavations and Preservation == In 1964, an excavation led by [[Osaka University]] unearthed approximately 2,000 artifacts.<ref>"Crowdfunding Advances for 3D Digitalization of Important Artifacts," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' December 22, 2018.</ref> The site was designated as a National Historic Site in 1995 and incorporated into the unified designation of the "Furuichi Kofun Cluster" in 2001. The iron armor and other artifacts are stored at Osaka University and were designated as Important Cultural Properties of Japan in 2015.<ref>"Osaka City Art Museum Registered as Cultural Property," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' March 15, 2015.</ref>
Additionally, a 1964 color film documenting the excavation was rediscovered in 2009.<ref>"1964 Nonaka Kofun Excavation Film Found," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' December 24, 2012.</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Literature == * ''Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia,'' Tokyo-dō Shuppan, 1989.
== Related Sites == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} * {{ill|Nonaka Ariyama Kofun|ja|野中アリ山古墳}} * {{ill|Katombo-yama Kofun|ja|カトンボ山古墳}} - A baizuka featuring similar abundant burial goods, including imitation stone products and beads.
== External Links == * [http://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/10kakuka/34shakaikyoiku/03bunkazai/04isekishokai/13kaisetsu/k_nonaka.html Nonaka Kofun] (Habikino City Website) * [http://www.let.osaka-u.ac.jp/kouko/nonaka/ Nonaka Kofun] (Osaka University Archaeology Laboratory)

{{Infobox Kofun | name = Nonaka Kofun | native_name = 野中古墳 | location = {{ill|Fujiidera, Osaka|ja|藤井寺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|33|36.9|N|135|36|9.3|E|display=inline,title}} | region = Japan | type = {{ill|Square tumulus (Hōfun)|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} | length = 37m per side | built = 5th century | burial = Wooden coffin, wooden chest | artifacts = Armor, iron weapons | designated = National [[Historic Site of Japan|Historic Site]] (part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}) | map = Japan Osaka | map_caption = Location in Osaka Prefecture | image = {{image requested|cat=Fujiidera, Osaka}} }} '''Nonaka Kofun''' (野中古墳) is a mid-[[Kofun period]] burial mound ([[kofun]]) located in {{ill|Fujidera|ja|藤井寺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. {{Infobox Kofun | name = Nonaka Kofun | native_name = 野中古墳 | location = {{ill|Fujiidera, Osaka|ja|藤井寺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|33|36.9|N|135|36|9.3|E|display=inline,title}} | region = Japan | type = {{ill|Square tumulus (Hōfun)|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} | length = 37m per side | built = 5th century | burial = Wooden coffin, wooden chest | artifacts = Armor, iron weapons | designated = National [[Historic Site of Japan|Historic Site]] (part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}) | map = Japan Osaka | map_caption = Location in Osaka Prefecture | image = {{image requested|cat=Fujiidera, Osaka}} }} '''Nonaka Kofun''' (野中古墳) is a mid-[[Kofun period]] burial mound ([[kofun]]) located in {{ill|Fujidera|ja|藤井寺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. A significant number of iron weapons, armor, and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} have been excavated from the site. The kofun is designated as a part of the [[UNESCO]] [[World Cultural Heritage]] "Ancient Tumulus Clusters," specifically within the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}}. It is also a [[National Historic Site of Japan]].
== Overview == The Nonaka Kofun is part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} and is situated north of the rear circle of the {{ill|Makasuyama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳|1=Hakayama Kofun}}, leading to speculation that it may have served as a {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|subsidiary burial mound}} for the latter.<ref name="yomiuri140212">"Restoration Completed for Nonaka Kofun Armor," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' February 12, 2014.</ref>
The mound is a square-shaped tumulus (方墳) built in two tiers, with each side measuring 37 meters and a height of 4 meters. It features a surrounding moat, {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (roofing stones), cylindrical haniwa rows, and figurative haniwa, including helmets, "Kinugasa" lids, and horned helmets (衝角付冑). Numerous {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} were excavated from the mound's summit.<ref name="yomiuri140212"/>
Beneath the mound's summit, five sets of burial facilities were aligned in parallel, consisting of wooden coffins and wooden chests. Only the second wooden coffin is believed to have contained human remains, while the other four are interpreted as dedicated for grave goods. These facilities yielded a significant collection of grave goods, including: * 11 sets of iron armor ([[tankō]] 短甲) * 11 helmets (冑) * Components such as grass skirts and shoulder guards * Iron swords (鉄刀), iron spears (鉄剣), and a large number of iron arrowheads (鉄鏃).
The abundant iron artifacts, Sue ware, and weapons provide invaluable insights into the burial practices of the mid-Kofun period. The tumulus is estimated to have been built during the early to mid-5th century.
== Excavations and Preservation == In 1964, an excavation led by [[Osaka University]] unearthed approximately 2,000 artifacts.<ref>"Crowdfunding Advances for 3D Digitalization of Important Artifacts," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' December 22, 2018.</ref> The site was designated as a National Historic Site in 1995 and incorporated into the unified designation of the "Furuichi Kofun Cluster" in 2001. The iron armor and other artifacts are stored at Osaka University and were designated as Important Cultural Properties of Japan in 2015.<ref>"Osaka City Art Museum Registered as Cultural Property," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' March 15, 2015.</ref>
Additionally, a 1964 color film documenting the excavation was rediscovered in 2009.<ref>"1964 Nonaka Kofun Excavation Film Found," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' December 24, 2012.</ref>

{{Infobox Kofun | name = Nonaka Kofun | native_name = 野中古墳 | location = {{ill|Fujiidera, Osaka|ja|藤井寺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|33|36.9|N|135|36|9.3|E|display=inline,title}} | region = Japan | type = {{ill|Square tumulus (Hōfun)|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} | length = 37m per side | built = 5th century | burial = Wooden coffin, wooden chest | artifacts = Armor, iron weapons | designated = National [[Historic Site of Japan|Historic Site]] (part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}) | map = Japan Osaka | map_caption = Location in Osaka Prefecture | image = {{image requested|cat=Fujiidera, Osaka}} }} '''Nonaka Kofun''' (野中古墳) is a mid-[[Kofun period]] burial mound ([[kofun]]) located in {{ill|Fujidera|ja|藤井寺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. A significant number of iron weapons, armor, and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} have been excavated from the site. The kofun is designated as a part of the [[UNESCO]] [[World Cultural Heritage]] "Ancient Tumulus Clusters," specifically within the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}}. It is also a [[National Historic Site of Japan]].
== Overview == The Nonaka Kofun is part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} and is situated north of the rear circle of the {{ill|Makasuyama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳|1=Hakayama Kofun}}, leading to speculation that it may have served as a {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|subsidiary burial mound}} for the latter.<ref name="yomiuri140212">"Restoration Completed for Nonaka Kofun Armor," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' February 12, 2014.</ref>
The mound is a square-shaped tumulus (方墳) built in two tiers, with each side measuring 37 meters and a height of 4 meters. It features a surrounding moat, {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (roofing stones), cylindrical haniwa rows, and figurative haniwa, including helmets, "Kinugasa" lids, and horned helmets (衝角付冑). Numerous {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} were excavated from the mound's summit.<ref name="yomiuri140212"/>
Beneath the mound's summit, five sets of burial facilities were aligned in parallel, consisting of wooden coffins and wooden chests. Only the second wooden coffin is believed to have contained human remains, while the other four are interpreted as dedicated for grave goods. These facilities yielded a significant collection of grave goods, including: * 11 sets of iron armor ([[tankō]] 短甲) * 11 helmets (冑) * Components such as grass skirts and shoulder guards * Iron swords (鉄刀), iron spears (鉄剣), and a large number of iron arrowheads (鉄鏃).
The abundant iron artifacts, Sue ware, and weapons provide invaluable insights into the burial practices of the mid-Kofun period. The tumulus is estimated to have been built during the early to mid-5th century.
== Excavations and Preservation == In 1964, an excavation led by [[Osaka University]] unearthed approximately 2,000 artifacts.<ref>"Crowdfunding Advances for 3D Digitalization of Important Artifacts," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' December 22, 2018.</ref> The site was designated as a National Historic Site in 1995 and incorporated into the unified designation of the "Furuichi Kofun Cluster" in 2001. The iron armor and other artifacts are stored at Osaka University and were designated as Important Cultural Properties of Japan in 2015.<ref>"Osaka City Art Museum Registered as Cultural Property," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' March 15, 2015.</ref>
Additionally, a 1964 color film documenting the excavation was rediscovered in 2009.<ref>"1964 Nonaka Kofun Excavation Film Found," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' December 24, 2012.</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Literature == * ''Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia,'' Tokyo-dō Shuppan, 1989.
== Related Sites == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} * {{ill|Nonaka Ariyama Kofun|ja|野中アリ山古墳}} * {{ill|Katombo-yama Kofun|ja|カトンボ山古墳}} - A baizuka featuring similar abundant burial goods, including imitation stone products and beads.
== External Links == * [http://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/10kakuka/34shakaikyoiku/03bunkazai/04isekishokai/13kaisetsu/k_nonaka.html Nonaka Kofun] (Habikino City Website) * [http://www.let.osaka-u.ac.jp/kouko/nonaka/ Nonaka Kofun] (Osaka University Archaeology Laboratory)

{{Infobox Kofun | name = Nonaka Kofun | native_name = 野中古墳 | location = {{ill|Fujiidera, Osaka|ja|藤井寺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|33|36.9|N|135|36|9.3|E|display=inline,title}} | region = Japan | type = {{ill|Square tumulus (Hōfun)|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} | length = 37m per side | built = 5th century | burial = Wooden coffin, wooden chest | artifacts = Armor, iron weapons | designated = National [[Historic Site of Japan|Historic Site]] (part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}) | map = Japan Osaka | map_caption = Location in Osaka Prefecture | image = {{image requested|cat=Fujiidera, Osaka}} }} '''Nonaka Kofun''' (野中古墳) is a mid-[[Kofun period]] burial mound ([[kofun]]) located in {{ill|Fujidera|ja|藤井寺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. {{Infobox Kofun | name = Nonaka Kofun | native_name = 野中古墳 | location = {{ill|Fujiidera, Osaka|ja|藤井寺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|33|36.9|N|135|36|9.3|E|display=inline,title}} | region = Japan | type = {{ill|Square tumulus (Hōfun)|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} | length = 37m per side | built = 5th century | burial = Wooden coffin, wooden chest | artifacts = Armor, iron weapons | designated = National [[Historic Site of Japan|Historic Site]] (part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}) | map = Japan Osaka | map_caption = Location in Osaka Prefecture | image = {{image requested|cat=Fujiidera, Osaka}} }} '''Nonaka Kofun''' (野中古墳) is a mid-[[Kofun period]] burial mound ([[kofun]]) located in {{ill|Fujidera|ja|藤井寺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. A significant number of iron weapons, armor, and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} have been excavated from the site. The kofun is designated as a part of the [[UNESCO]] [[World Cultural Heritage]] "Ancient Tumulus Clusters," specifically within the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}}. It is also a [[National Historic Site of Japan]].
== Overview == The Nonaka Kofun is part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} and is situated north of the rear circle of the {{ill|Makasuyama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳|1=Hakayama Kofun}}, leading to speculation that it may have served as a {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|subsidiary burial mound}} for the latter.<ref name="yomiuri140212">"Restoration Completed for Nonaka Kofun Armor," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' February 12, 2014.</ref>
The mound is a square-shaped tumulus (方墳) built in two tiers, with each side measuring 37 meters and a height of 4 meters. It features a surrounding moat, {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (roofing stones), cylindrical haniwa rows, and figurative haniwa, including helmets, "Kinugasa" lids, and horned helmets (衝角付冑). Numerous {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} were excavated from the mound's summit.<ref name="yomiuri140212"/>
Beneath the mound's summit, five sets of burial facilities were aligned in parallel, consisting of wooden coffins and wooden chests. Only the second wooden coffin is believed to have contained human remains, while the other four are interpreted as dedicated for grave goods. These facilities yielded a significant collection of grave goods, including: * 11 sets of iron armor ([[tankō]] 短甲) * 11 helmets (冑) * Components such as grass skirts and shoulder guards * Iron swords (鉄刀), iron spears (鉄剣), and a large number of iron arrowheads (鉄鏃).
The abundant iron artifacts, Sue ware, and weapons provide invaluable insights into the burial practices of the mid-Kofun period. The tumulus is estimated to have been built during the early to mid-5th century.
== Excavations and Preservation == In 1964, an excavation led by [[Osaka University]] unearthed approximately 2,000 artifacts.<ref>"Crowdfunding Advances for 3D Digitalization of Important Artifacts," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' December 22, 2018.</ref> The site was designated as a National Historic Site in 1995 and incorporated into the unified designation of the "Furuichi Kofun Cluster" in 2001. The iron armor and other artifacts are stored at Osaka University and were designated as Important Cultural Properties of Japan in 2015.<ref>"Osaka City Art Museum Registered as Cultural Property," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' March 15, 2015.</ref>
Additionally, a 1964 color film documenting the excavation was rediscovered in 2009.<ref>"1964 Nonaka Kofun Excavation Film Found," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' December 24, 2012.</ref>

{{Infobox Kofun | name = Nonaka Kofun | native_name = 野中古墳 | location = {{ill|Fujiidera, Osaka|ja|藤井寺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|33|36.9|N|135|36|9.3|E|display=inline,title}} | region = Japan | type = {{ill|Square tumulus (Hōfun)|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} | length = 37m per side | built = 5th century | burial = Wooden coffin, wooden chest | artifacts = Armor, iron weapons | designated = National [[Historic Site of Japan|Historic Site]] (part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}) | map = Japan Osaka | map_caption = Location in Osaka Prefecture | image = {{image requested|cat=Fujiidera, Osaka}} }} '''Nonaka Kofun''' (野中古墳) is a mid-[[Kofun period]] burial mound ([[kofun]]) located in {{ill|Fujidera|ja|藤井寺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. A significant number of iron weapons, armor, and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} have been excavated from the site. The kofun is designated as a part of the [[UNESCO]] [[World Cultural Heritage]] "Ancient Tumulus Clusters," specifically within the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}}. It is also a [[National Historic Site of Japan]].
== Overview == The Nonaka Kofun is part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} and is situated north of the rear circle of the {{ill|Makasuyama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳|1=Hakayama Kofun}}, leading to speculation that it may have served as a {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|subsidiary burial mound}} for the latter.<ref name="yomiuri140212">"Restoration Completed for Nonaka Kofun Armor," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' February 12, 2014.</ref>
The mound is a square-shaped tumulus (方墳) built in two tiers, with each side measuring 37 meters and a height of 4 meters. It features a surrounding moat, {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (roofing stones), cylindrical haniwa rows, and figurative haniwa, including helmets, "Kinugasa" lids, and horned helmets (衝角付冑). Numerous {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} were excavated from the mound's summit.<ref name="yomiuri140212"/>
Beneath the mound's summit, five sets of burial facilities were aligned in parallel, consisting of wooden coffins and wooden chests. Only the second wooden coffin is believed to have contained human remains, while the other four are interpreted as dedicated for grave goods. These facilities yielded a significant collection of grave goods, including: * 11 sets of iron armor ([[tankō]] 短甲) * 11 helmets (冑) * Components such as grass skirts and shoulder guards * Iron swords (鉄刀), iron spears (鉄剣), and a large number of iron arrowheads (鉄鏃).
The abundant iron artifacts, Sue ware, and weapons provide invaluable insights into the burial practices of the mid-Kofun period. The tumulus is estimated to have been built during the early to mid-5th century.
== Excavations and Preservation == In 1964, an excavation led by [[Osaka University]] unearthed approximately 2,000 artifacts.<ref>"Crowdfunding Advances for 3D Digitalization of Important Artifacts," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' December 22, 2018.</ref> The site was designated as a National Historic Site in 1995 and incorporated into the unified designation of the "Furuichi Kofun Cluster" in 2001. The iron armor and other artifacts are stored at Osaka University and were designated as Important Cultural Properties of Japan in 2015.<ref>"Osaka City Art Museum Registered as Cultural Property," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' March 15, 2015.</ref>
Additionally, a 1964 color film documenting the excavation was rediscovered in 2009.<ref>"1964 Nonaka Kofun Excavation Film Found," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' December 24, 2012.</ref>

{{Infobox Kofun | name = Nonaka Kofun | native_name = 野中古墳 | location = {{ill|Fujiidera, Osaka|ja|藤井寺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|33|36.9|N|135|36|9.3|E|display=inline,title}} | region = Japan | type = {{ill|Square tumulus (Hōfun)|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} | length = 37m per side | built = 5th century | burial = Wooden coffin, wooden chest | artifacts = Armor, iron weapons | designated = National [[Historic Site of Japan|Historic Site]] (part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}) | map = Japan Osaka | map_caption = Location in Osaka Prefecture | image = {{image requested|cat=Fujiidera, Osaka}} }} '''Nonaka Kofun''' (野中古墳) is a mid-[[Kofun period]] burial mound ([[kofun]]) located in {{ill|Fujidera|ja|藤井寺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. A significant number of iron weapons, armor, and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} have been excavated from the site. The kofun is designated as a part of the [[UNESCO]] [[World Cultural Heritage]] "Ancient Tumulus Clusters," specifically within the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}}. It is also a [[National Historic Site of Japan]].
== Overview == The Nonaka Kofun is part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} and is situated north of the rear circle of the {{ill|Makasuyama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳|1=Hakayama Kofun}}, leading to speculation that it may have served as a {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|subsidiary burial mound}} for the latter.<ref name="yomiuri140212">"Restoration Completed for Nonaka Kofun Armor," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' February 12, 2014.</ref>
The mound is a square-shaped tumulus (方墳) built in two tiers, with each side measuring 37 meters and a height of 4 meters. It features a surrounding moat, {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (roofing stones), cylindrical haniwa rows, and figurative haniwa, including helmets, "Kinugasa" lids, and horned helmets (衝角付冑). Numerous {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} were excavated from the mound's summit.<ref name="yomiuri140212"/>
Beneath the mound's summit, five sets of burial facilities were aligned in parallel, consisting of wooden coffins and wooden chests. Only the second wooden coffin is believed to have contained human remains, while the other four are interpreted as dedicated for grave goods. These facilities yielded a significant collection of grave goods, including: * 11 sets of iron armor ([[tankō]] 短甲) * 11 helmets (冑) * Components such as grass skirts and shoulder guards * Iron swords (鉄刀), iron spears (鉄剣), and a large number of iron arrowheads (鉄鏃).
The abundant iron artifacts, Sue ware, and weapons provide invaluable insights into the burial practices of the mid-Kofun period. The tumulus is estimated to have been built during the early to mid-5th century.
== Excavations and Preservation == In 1964, an excavation led by [[Osaka University]] unearthed approximately 2,000 artifacts.<ref>"Crowdfunding Advances for 3D Digitalization of Important Artifacts," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' December 22, 2018.</ref> The site was designated as a National Historic Site in 1995 and incorporated into the unified designation of the "Furuichi Kofun Cluster" in 2001. The iron armor and other artifacts are stored at Osaka University and were designated as Important Cultural Properties of Japan in 2015.<ref>"Osaka City Art Museum Registered as Cultural Property," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' March 15, 2015.</ref>
Additionally, a 1964 color film documenting the excavation was rediscovered in 2009.<ref>"1964 Nonaka Kofun Excavation Film Found," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' December 24, 2012.</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Literature == * ''Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia,'' Tokyo-dō Shuppan, 1989.
== Related Sites == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} * {{ill|Nonaka Ariyama Kofun|ja|野中アリ山古墳}} * {{ill|Katombo-yama Kofun|ja|カトンボ山古墳}} - A baizuka featuring similar abundant burial goods, including imitation stone products and beads.
== External Links == * [http://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/10kakuka/34shakaikyoiku/03bunkazai/04isekishokai/13kaisetsu/k_nonaka.html Nonaka Kofun] (Habikino City Website) * [http://www.let.osaka-u.ac.jp/kouko/nonaka/ Nonaka Kofun] (Osaka University Archaeology Laboratory)

{{Infobox Kofun | name = Nonaka Kofun | native_name = 野中古墳 | location = {{ill|Fujiidera, Osaka|ja|藤井寺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|33|36.9|N|135|36|9.3|E|display=inline,title}} | region = Japan | type = {{ill|Square tumulus (Hōfun)|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} | length = 37m per side | built = 5th century | burial = Wooden coffin, wooden chest | artifacts = Armor, iron weapons | designated = National [[Historic Site of Japan|Historic Site]] (part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}) | map = Japan Osaka | map_caption = Location in Osaka Prefecture | image = {{image requested|cat=Fujiidera, Osaka}} }} '''Nonaka Kofun''' (野中古墳) is a mid-[[Kofun period]] Kofun ([[kofun]]) located in {{ill|Fujidera|ja|藤井寺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. {{Infobox Kofun | name = Nonaka Kofun | native_name = 野中古墳 | location = {{ill|Fujiidera, Osaka|ja|藤井寺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|33|36.9|N|135|36|9.3|E|display=inline,title}} | region = Japan | type = {{ill|Square tumulus (Hōfun)|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} | length = 37m per side | built = 5th century | burial = Wooden coffin, wooden chest | artifacts = Armor, iron weapons | designated = National [[Historic Site of Japan|Historic Site]] (part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}) | map = Japan Osaka | map_caption = Location in Osaka Prefecture | image = {{image requested|cat=Fujiidera, Osaka}} }} '''Nonaka Kofun''' (野中古墳) is a mid-[[Kofun period]] Kofun ([[kofun]]) located in {{ill|Fujidera|ja|藤井寺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. A significant number of iron weapons, armor, and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} have been excavated from the site. The kofun is designated as a part of the [[UNESCO]] [[World Cultural Heritage]] "Ancient Kofun Clusters," specifically within the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}}. It is also a [[National Historic Site of Japan]].
== Overview == The Nonaka Kofun is part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} and is situated north of the rear circle of the {{ill|Makasuyama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳|1=Hakayama Kofun}}, leading to speculation that it may have served as a {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|subsidiary Kofun}} for the latter.<ref name="yomiuri140212">"Restoration Completed for Nonaka Kofun Armor," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' February 12, 2014.</ref>
The mound is a square-shaped tumulus (方墳) built in two tiers, with each side measuring 37 meters and a height of 4 meters. It features a surrounding moat, {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (roofing stones), cylindrical haniwa rows, and figurative haniwa, including helmets, "Kinugasa" lids, and horned helmets (衝角付冑). Numerous {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} were excavated from the mound's summit.<ref name="yomiuri140212"/>
Beneath the mound's summit, five sets of burial facilities were aligned in parallel, consisting of wooden coffins and wooden chests. Only the second wooden coffin is believed to have contained human remains, while the other four are interpreted as dedicated for grave goods. These facilities yielded a significant collection of grave goods, including: * 11 sets of iron armor ([[tankō]] 短甲) * 11 helmets (冑) * Components such as grass skirts and shoulder guards * Iron swords (鉄刀), iron spears (鉄剣), and a large number of iron arrowheads (鉄鏃).
The abundant iron artifacts, Sue ware, and weapons provide invaluable insights into the burial practices of the mid-Kofun period. The tumulus is estimated to have been built during the early to mid-5th century.
== Excavations and Preservation == In 1964, an excavation led by [[Osaka University]] unearthed approximately 2,000 artifacts.<ref>"Crowdfunding Advances for 3D Digitalization of Important Artifacts," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' December 22, 2018.</ref> The site was designated as a National Historic Site in 1995 and incorporated into the unified designation of the "Furuichi Kofun Cluster" in 2001. The iron armor and other artifacts are stored at Osaka University and were designated as Important Cultural Properties of Japan in 2015.<ref>"Osaka City Art Museum Registered as Cultural Property," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' March 15, 2015.</ref>
Additionally, a 1964 color film documenting the excavation was rediscovered in 2009.<ref>"1964 Nonaka Kofun Excavation Film Found," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' December 24, 2012.</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Literature == * ''Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia,'' Tokyo-dō Shuppan, 1989.
== Related Sites == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} * {{ill|Nonaka Ariyama Kofun|ja|野中アリ山古墳}} * {{ill|Katombo-yama Kofun|ja|カトンボ山古墳}} - A baizuka featuring similar abundant burial goods, including imitation stone products and beads.
== External Links == * [http://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/10kakuka/34shakaikyoiku/03bunkazai/04isekishokai/13kaisetsu/k_nonaka.html Nonaka Kofun] (Habikino City Website) * [http://www.let.osaka-u.ac.jp/kouko/nonaka/ Nonaka Kofun] (Osaka University Archaeology Laboratory)

revision.text:
{{Infobox Kofun | name = Nonaka Kofun | native_name = 野中古墳 | location = {{ill|Fujiidera, Osaka|ja|藤井寺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|33|36.9|N|135|36|9.3|E|display=inline,title}} | region = Japan | type = {{ill|Square tumulus (Hōfun)|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} | length = 37m per side | built = 5th century | burial = Wooden coffin, wooden chest | artifacts = Armor, iron weapons | designated = National [[Historic Site of Japan|Historic Site]] (part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}) | map = Japan Osaka | map_caption = Location in Osaka Prefecture | image = {{image requested|cat=Fujiidera, Osaka}} }} '''Nonaka Kofun''' (野中古墳) is a mid-[[Kofun period]] Kofun ([[kofun]]) located in {{ill|Fujidera|ja|藤井寺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. A significant number of iron weapons, armor, and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} have been excavated from the site. The kofun is designated as a part of the [[UNESCO]] [[World Cultural Heritage]] "Ancient Kofun Clusters," specifically within the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}}. It is also a [[National Historic Site of Japan]].
== Overview == The Nonaka Kofun is part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} and is situated north of the rear circle of the {{ill|Makasuyama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳|1=Hakayama Kofun}}, leading to speculation that it may have served as a {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|subsidiary Kofun}} for the latter.<ref name="yomiuri140212">"Restoration Completed for Nonaka Kofun Armor," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' February 12, 2014.</ref>
The mound is a square-shaped tumulus (方墳) built in two tiers, with each side measuring 37 meters and a height of 4 meters. It features a surrounding moat, {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (roofing stones), cylindrical haniwa rows, and figurative haniwa, including helmets, "Kinugasa" lids, and horned helmets (衝角付冑). Numerous {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} were excavated from the mound's summit.<ref name="yomiuri140212"/>
Beneath the mound's summit, five sets of burial facilities were aligned in parallel, consisting of wooden coffins and wooden chests. Only the second wooden coffin is believed to have contained human remains, while the other four are interpreted as dedicated for grave goods. These facilities yielded a significant collection of grave goods, including: * 11 sets of iron armor ([[tankō]] 短甲) * 11 helmets (冑) * Components such as grass skirts and shoulder guards * Iron swords (鉄刀), iron spears (鉄剣), and a large number of iron arrowheads (鉄鏃).
The abundant iron artifacts, Sue ware, and weapons provide invaluable insights into the burial practices of the mid-Kofun period. The tumulus is estimated to have been built during the early to mid-5th century.
== Excavations and Preservation == In 1964, an excavation led by [[Osaka University]] unearthed approximately 2,000 artifacts.<ref>"Crowdfunding Advances for 3D Digitalization of Important Artifacts," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' December 22, 2018.</ref> The site was designated as a National Historic Site in 1995 and incorporated into the unified designation of the "Furuichi Kofun Cluster" in 2001. The iron armor and other artifacts are stored at Osaka University and were designated as Important Cultural Properties of Japan in 2015.<ref>"Osaka City Art Museum Registered as Cultural Property," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' March 15, 2015.</ref>
Additionally, a 1964 color film documenting the excavation was rediscovered in 2009.<ref>"1964 Nonaka Kofun Excavation Film Found," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' December 24, 2012.</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Literature == * ''Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia,'' Tokyo-dō Shuppan, 1989.
== Related Sites == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} * {{ill|Nonaka Ariyama Kofun|ja|野中アリ山古墳}} * {{ill|Katombo-yama Kofun|ja|カトンボ山古墳}} - A baizuka featuring similar abundant burial goods, including imitation stone products and beads.
== External Links == * [http://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/10kakuka/34shakaikyoiku/03bunkazai/04isekishokai/13kaisetsu/k_nonaka.html Nonaka Kofun] (Habikino City Website) * [http://www.let.osaka-u.ac.jp/kouko/nonaka/ Nonaka Kofun] (Osaka University Archaeology Laboratory)

revision.comment: Moving from [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] to [[Category:completion tier 5]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox Kofun | name = Nonaka Kofun | native_name = 野中古墳 | location = {{ill|Fujiidera, Osaka|ja|藤井寺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|33|36.9|N|135|36|9.3|E|display=inline,title}} | region = Japan | type = {{ill|Square tumulus (Hōfun)|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} | length = 37m per side | built = 5th century | burial = Wooden coffin, wooden chest | artifacts = Armor, iron weapons | designated = National [[Historic Site of Japan|Historic Site]] (part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}) | map = Japan Osaka | map_caption = Location in Osaka Prefecture | image = {{image requested|cat=Fujiidera, Osaka}} }} '''Nonaka Kofun''' (野中古墳) is a mid-[[Kofun period]] Kofun ([[kofun]]) located in {{ill|Fujidera|ja|藤井寺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. A significant number of iron weapons, armor, and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} have been excavated from the site. The kofun is designated as a part of the [[UNESCO]] [[World Cultural Heritage]] "Ancient Kofun Clusters," specifically within the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}}. It is also a [[National Historic Site of Japan]].
== Overview == The Nonaka Kofun is part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} and is situated north of the rear circle of the {{ill|Makasuyama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳|1=Hakayama Kofun}}, leading to speculation that it may have served as a {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|subsidiary Kofun}} for the latter.<ref name="yomiuri140212">"Restoration Completed for Nonaka Kofun Armor," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' February 12, 2014.</ref>
The mound is a square-shaped tumulus (方墳) built in two tiers, with each side measuring 37 meters and a height of 4 meters. It features a surrounding moat, {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (roofing stones), cylindrical haniwa rows, and figurative haniwa, including helmets, "Kinugasa" lids, and horned helmets (衝角付冑). Numerous {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} were excavated from the mound's summit.<ref name="yomiuri140212"/>
Beneath the mound's summit, five sets of burial facilities were aligned in parallel, consisting of wooden coffins and wooden chests. Only the second wooden coffin is believed to have contained human remains, while the other four are interpreted as dedicated for grave goods. These facilities yielded a significant collection of grave goods, including: * 11 sets of iron armor ([[tankō]] 短甲) * 11 helmets (冑) * Components such as grass skirts and shoulder guards * Iron swords (鉄刀), iron spears (鉄剣), and a large number of iron arrowheads (鉄鏃).
The abundant iron artifacts, Sue ware, and weapons provide invaluable insights into the burial practices of the mid-Kofun period. The tumulus is estimated to have been built during the early to mid-5th century.
== Excavations and Preservation == In 1964, an excavation led by [[Osaka University]] unearthed approximately 2,000 artifacts.<ref>"Crowdfunding Advances for 3D Digitalization of Important Artifacts," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' December 22, 2018.</ref> The site was designated as a National Historic Site in 1995 and incorporated into the unified designation of the "Furuichi Kofun Cluster" in 2001. The iron armor and other artifacts are stored at Osaka University and were designated as Important Cultural Properties of Japan in 2015.<ref>"Osaka City Art Museum Registered as Cultural Property," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' March 15, 2015.</ref>
Additionally, a 1964 color film documenting the excavation was rediscovered in 2009.<ref>"1964 Nonaka Kofun Excavation Film Found," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' December 24, 2012.</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Literature == * ''Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia,'' Tokyo-dō Shuppan, 1989.
== Related Sites == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} * {{ill|Nonaka Ariyama Kofun|ja|野中アリ山古墳}} * {{ill|Katombo-yama Kofun|ja|カトンボ山古墳}} - A baizuka featuring similar abundant burial goods, including imitation stone products and beads.
== External Links == * [http://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/10kakuka/34shakaikyoiku/03bunkazai/04isekishokai/13kaisetsu/k_nonaka.html Nonaka Kofun] (Habikino City Website) * [http://www.let.osaka-u.ac.jp/kouko/nonaka/ Nonaka Kofun] (Osaka University Archaeology Laboratory)

== Excavations and Preservation == In 1964, an excavation led by [[Osaka University]] unearthed approximately 2,000 artifacts.<ref>"Crowdfunding Advances for 3D Digitalization of Important Artifacts," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' December 22, 2018.</ref> The site was designated as a National Historic Site in 1995 and incorporated into the unified designation of the "Furuichi Kofun Cluster" in 2001. The iron armor and other artifacts are stored at Osaka University and were designated as Important Cultural Properties of Japan in 2015.<ref>"Osaka City Art Museum Registered as Cultural Property," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' March 15, 2015.</ref>
Additionally, a 1964 color film documenting the excavation was rediscovered in 2009.<ref>"1964 Nonaka Kofun Excavation Film Found," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' December 24, 2012.</ref>

{{Infobox Kofun | name = Nonaka Kofun | native_name = 野中古墳 | location = {{ill|Fujiidera, Osaka|ja|藤井寺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|33|36.9|N|135|36|9.3|E|display=inline,title}} | region = Japan | type = {{ill|Square tumulus (Hōfun)|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} | length = 37m per side | built = 5th century | burial = Wooden coffin, wooden chest | artifacts = Armor, iron weapons | designated = National [[Historic Site of Japan|Historic Site]] (part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}) | map = Japan Osaka | map_caption = Location in Osaka Prefecture | image = {{image requested|cat=Fujiidera, Osaka}} }} '''Nonaka Kofun''' (野中古墳) is a mid-[[Kofun period]] Kofun ([[kofun]]) located in {{ill|Fujidera|ja|藤井寺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. A significant number of iron weapons, armor, and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} have been excavated from the site. The kofun is designated as a part of the [[UNESCO]] [[World Cultural Heritage]] "Ancient Kofun Clusters," specifically within the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}}. It is also a [[National Historic Site of Japan]].
== Overview == The Nonaka Kofun is part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} and is situated north of the rear circle of the {{ill|Makasuyama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳|1=Hakayama Kofun}}, leading to speculation that it may have served as a {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|subsidiary Kofun}} for the latter.<ref name="yomiuri140212">"Restoration Completed for Nonaka Kofun Armor," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' February 12, 2014.</ref>
The mound is a square-shaped tumulus (方墳) built in two tiers, with each side measuring 37 meters and a height of 4 meters. It features a surrounding moat, {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (roofing stones), cylindrical haniwa rows, and figurative haniwa, including helmets, "Kinugasa" lids, and horned helmets (衝角付冑). Numerous {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} were excavated from the mound's summit.<ref name="yomiuri140212"/>
Beneath the mound's summit, five sets of burial facilities were aligned in parallel, consisting of wooden coffins and wooden chests. Only the second wooden coffin is believed to have contained human remains, while the other four are interpreted as dedicated for grave goods. These facilities yielded a significant collection of grave goods, including: * 11 sets of iron armor ([[tankō]] 短甲) * 11 helmets (冑) * Components such as grass skirts and shoulder guards * Iron swords (鉄刀), iron spears (鉄剣), and a large number of iron arrowheads (鉄鏃).
The abundant iron artifacts, Sue ware, and weapons provide invaluable insights into the burial practices of the mid-Kofun period. The tumulus is estimated to have been built during the early to mid-5th century.
== Excavations and Preservation == In 1964, an excavation led by [[Osaka University]] unearthed approximately 2,000 artifacts.<ref>"Crowdfunding Advances for 3D Digitalization of Important Artifacts," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' December 22, 2018.</ref> The site was designated as a National Historic Site in 1995 and incorporated into the unified designation of the "Furuichi Kofun Cluster" in 2001. The iron armor and other artifacts are stored at Osaka University and were designated as Important Cultural Properties of Japan in 2015.<ref>"Osaka City Art Museum Registered as Cultural Property," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' March 15, 2015.</ref>
Additionally, a 1964 color film documenting the excavation was rediscovered in 2009.<ref>"1964 Nonaka Kofun Excavation Film Found," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' December 24, 2012.</ref>

The mound is a square-shaped tumulus (方墳) built in two tiers, with each side measuring 37 meters and a height of 4 meters. It features a surrounding moat, {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (roofing stones), cylindrical haniwa rows, and figurative haniwa, including helmets, "Kinugasa" lids, and horned helmets (衝角付冑). Numerous {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} were excavated from the mound's summit.<ref name="yomiuri140212"/>
Beneath the mound's summit, five sets of burial facilities were aligned in parallel, consisting of wooden coffins and wooden chests. Only the second wooden coffin is believed to have contained human remains, while the other four are interpreted as dedicated for grave goods. These facilities yielded a significant collection of grave goods, including: * 11 sets of iron armor ([[tankō]] 短甲) * 11 helmets (冑) * Components such as grass skirts and shoulder guards * Iron swords (鉄刀), iron spears (鉄剣), and a large number of iron arrowheads (鉄鏃).
The abundant iron artifacts, Sue ware, and weapons provide invaluable insights into the burial practices of the mid-Kofun period. The tumulus is estimated to have been built during the early to mid-5th century.
== Excavations and Preservation == In 1964, an excavation led by [[Osaka University]] unearthed approximately 2,000 artifacts.<ref>"Crowdfunding Advances for 3D Digitalization of Important Artifacts," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' December 22, 2018.</ref> The site was designated as a National Historic Site in 1995 and incorporated into the unified designation of the "Furuichi Kofun Cluster" in 2001. The iron armor and other artifacts are stored at Osaka University and were designated as Important Cultural Properties of Japan in 2015.<ref>"Osaka City Art Museum Registered as Cultural Property," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' March 15, 2015.</ref>
Additionally, a 1964 color film documenting the excavation was rediscovered in 2009.<ref>"1964 Nonaka Kofun Excavation Film Found," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' December 24, 2012.</ref>

{{Infobox Kofun | name = Nonaka Kofun | native_name = 野中古墳 | location = {{ill|Fujiidera, Osaka|ja|藤井寺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|33|36.9|N|135|36|9.3|E|display=inline,title}} | region = Japan | type = {{ill|Square tumulus (Hōfun)|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} | length = 37m per side | built = 5th century | burial = Wooden coffin, wooden chest | artifacts = Armor, iron weapons | designated = National [[Historic Site of Japan|Historic Site]] (part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}) | map = Japan Osaka | map_caption = Location in Osaka Prefecture | image = {{image requested|cat=Fujiidera, Osaka}} }} '''Nonaka Kofun''' (野中古墳) is a mid-[[Kofun period]] Kofun ([[kofun]]) located in {{ill|Fujidera|ja|藤井寺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. A significant number of iron weapons, armor, and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} have been excavated from the site. The kofun is designated as a part of the [[UNESCO]] [[World Cultural Heritage]] "Ancient Kofun Clusters," specifically within the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}}. It is also a [[National Historic Site of Japan]].
== Overview == The Nonaka Kofun is part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} and is situated north of the rear circle of the {{ill|Makasuyama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳|1=Hakayama Kofun}}, leading to speculation that it may have served as a {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|subsidiary Kofun}} for the latter.<ref name="yomiuri140212">"Restoration Completed for Nonaka Kofun Armor," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' February 12, 2014.</ref>
The mound is a square-shaped tumulus (方墳) built in two tiers, with each side measuring 37 meters and a height of 4 meters. It features a surrounding moat, {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (roofing stones), cylindrical haniwa rows, and figurative haniwa, including helmets, "Kinugasa" lids, and horned helmets (衝角付冑). Numerous {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} were excavated from the mound's summit.<ref name="yomiuri140212"/>
Beneath the mound's summit, five sets of burial facilities were aligned in parallel, consisting of wooden coffins and wooden chests. Only the second wooden coffin is believed to have contained human remains, while the other four are interpreted as dedicated for grave goods. These facilities yielded a significant collection of grave goods, including: * 11 sets of iron armor ([[tankō]] 短甲) * 11 helmets (冑) * Components such as grass skirts and shoulder guards * Iron swords (鉄刀), iron spears (鉄剣), and a large number of iron arrowheads (鉄鏃).
The abundant iron artifacts, Sue ware, and weapons provide invaluable insights into the burial practices of the mid-Kofun period. The tumulus is estimated to have been built during the early to mid-5th century.
== Excavations and Preservation == In 1964, an excavation led by [[Osaka University]] unearthed approximately 2,000 artifacts.<ref>"Crowdfunding Advances for 3D Digitalization of Important Artifacts," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' December 22, 2018.</ref> The site was designated as a National Historic Site in 1995 and incorporated into the unified designation of the "Furuichi Kofun Cluster" in 2001. The iron armor and other artifacts are stored at Osaka University and were designated as Important Cultural Properties of Japan in 2015.<ref>"Osaka City Art Museum Registered as Cultural Property," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' March 15, 2015.</ref>
Additionally, a 1964 color film documenting the excavation was rediscovered in 2009.<ref>"1964 Nonaka Kofun Excavation Film Found," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' December 24, 2012.</ref>

Numerous {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} were excavated from the mound's summit.<ref name="yomiuri140212"/>
Beneath the mound's summit, five sets of burial facilities were aligned in parallel, consisting of wooden coffins and wooden chests. Only the second wooden coffin is believed to have contained human remains, while the other four are interpreted as dedicated for grave goods. These facilities yielded a significant collection of grave goods, including: * 11 sets of iron armor ([[tankō]] 短甲) * 11 helmets (冑) * Components such as grass skirts and shoulder guards * Iron swords (鉄刀), iron spears (鉄剣), and a large number of iron arrowheads (鉄鏃).
The abundant iron artifacts, Sue ware, and weapons provide invaluable insights into the burial practices of the mid-Kofun period. The tumulus is estimated to have been built during the early to mid-5th century.
== Excavations and Preservation == In 1964, an excavation led by [[Osaka University]] unearthed approximately 2,000 artifacts.<ref>"Crowdfunding Advances for 3D Digitalization of Important Artifacts," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' December 22, 2018.</ref> The site was designated as a National Historic Site in 1995 and incorporated into the unified designation of the "Furuichi Kofun Cluster" in 2001. The iron armor and other artifacts are stored at Osaka University and were designated as Important Cultural Properties of Japan in 2015.<ref>"Osaka City Art Museum Registered as Cultural Property," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' March 15, 2015.</ref>
Additionally, a 1964 color film documenting the excavation was rediscovered in 2009.<ref>"1964 Nonaka Kofun Excavation Film Found," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' December 24, 2012.</ref>

{{Infobox Kofun | name = Nonaka Kofun | native_name = 野中古墳 | location = {{ill|Fujiidera, Osaka|ja|藤井寺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|33|36.9|N|135|36|9.3|E|display=inline,title}} | region = Japan | type = {{ill|Square tumulus (Hōfun)|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} | length = 37m per side | built = 5th century | burial = Wooden coffin, wooden chest | artifacts = Armor, iron weapons | designated = National [[Historic Site of Japan|Historic Site]] (part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}) | map = Japan Osaka | map_caption = Location in Osaka Prefecture | image = {{image requested|cat=Fujiidera, Osaka}} }} '''Nonaka Kofun''' (野中古墳) is a mid-[[Kofun period]] Kofun ([[kofun]]) located in {{ill|Fujidera|ja|藤井寺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. A significant number of iron weapons, armor, and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} have been excavated from the site. The kofun is designated as a part of the [[UNESCO]] [[World Cultural Heritage]] "Ancient Kofun Clusters," specifically within the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}}. It is also a [[National Historic Site of Japan]].
== Overview == The Nonaka Kofun is part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} and is situated north of the rear circle of the {{ill|Makasuyama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳|1=Hakayama Kofun}}, leading to speculation that it may have served as a {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|subsidiary Kofun}} for the latter.<ref name="yomiuri140212">"Restoration Completed for Nonaka Kofun Armor," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' February 12, 2014.</ref>
The mound is a square-shaped tumulus (方墳) built in two tiers, with each side measuring 37 meters and a height of 4 meters. It features a surrounding moat, {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (roofing stones), cylindrical haniwa rows, and figurative haniwa, including helmets, "Kinugasa" lids, and horned helmets (衝角付冑). Numerous {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} were excavated from the mound's summit.<ref name="yomiuri140212"/>
Beneath the mound's summit, five sets of burial facilities were aligned in parallel, consisting of wooden coffins and wooden chests. Only the second wooden coffin is believed to have contained human remains, while the other four are interpreted as dedicated for grave goods. These facilities yielded a significant collection of grave goods, including: * 11 sets of iron armor ([[tankō]] 短甲) * 11 helmets (冑) * Components such as grass skirts and shoulder guards * Iron swords (鉄刀), iron spears (鉄剣), and a large number of iron arrowheads (鉄鏃).
The abundant iron artifacts, Sue ware, and weapons provide invaluable insights into the burial practices of the mid-Kofun period. The tumulus is estimated to have been built during the early to mid-5th century.
== Excavations and Preservation == In 1964, an excavation led by [[Osaka University]] unearthed approximately 2,000 artifacts.<ref>"Crowdfunding Advances for 3D Digitalization of Important Artifacts," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' December 22, 2018.</ref> The site was designated as a National Historic Site in 1995 and incorporated into the unified designation of the "Furuichi Kofun Cluster" in 2001. The iron armor and other artifacts are stored at Osaka University and were designated as Important Cultural Properties of Japan in 2015.<ref>"Osaka City Art Museum Registered as Cultural Property," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' March 15, 2015.</ref>
Additionally, a 1964 color film documenting the excavation was rediscovered in 2009.<ref>"1964 Nonaka Kofun Excavation Film Found," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' December 24, 2012.</ref>

{{Infobox Kofun | name = Nonaka Kofun | native_name = 野中古墳 | location = {{ill|Fujiidera, Osaka|ja|藤井寺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|33|36.9|N|135|36|9.3|E|display=inline,title}} | region = Japan | type = {{ill|Square tumulus (Hōfun)|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} | length = 37m per side | built = 5th century | burial = Wooden coffin, wooden chest | artifacts = Armor, iron weapons | designated = National [[Historic Site of Japan|Historic Site]] (part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}) | map = Japan Osaka | map_caption = Location in Osaka Prefecture | image = {{image requested|cat=Fujiidera, Osaka}} }} '''Nonaka Kofun''' (野中古墳) is a mid-[[Kofun period]] Kofun ([[kofun]]) located in {{ill|Fujidera|ja|藤井寺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. A significant number of iron weapons, armor, and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器}} have been excavated from the site. The kofun is designated as a part of the [[UNESCO]] [[World Cultural Heritage]] "Ancient Kofun Clusters," specifically within the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}}. It is also a [[National Historic Site of Japan]].
== Overview == The Nonaka Kofun is part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} and is situated north of the rear circle of the {{ill|Makasuyama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳|1=Hakayama Kofun}}, leading to speculation that it may have served as a {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|subsidiary Kofun}} for the latter.<ref name="yomiuri140212">"Restoration Completed for Nonaka Kofun Armor," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' February 12, 2014.</ref>
The mound is a square-shaped tumulus (方墳) built in two tiers, with each side measuring 37 meters and a height of 4 meters. It features a surrounding moat, {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (roofing stones), cylindrical haniwa rows, and figurative haniwa, including helmets, "Kinugasa" lids, and horned helmets (衝角付冑). Numerous {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器}} and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} were excavated from the mound's summit.<ref name="yomiuri140212"/>
Beneath the mound's summit, five sets of burial facilities were aligned in parallel, consisting of wooden coffins and wooden chests. Only the second wooden coffin is believed to have contained human remains, while the other four are interpreted as dedicated for grave goods. These facilities yielded a significant collection of grave goods, including: * 11 sets of iron armor ([[tankō]] 短甲) * 11 helmets (冑) * Components such as grass skirts and shoulder guards * Iron swords (鉄刀), iron spears (鉄剣), and a large number of iron arrowheads (鉄鏃).
The abundant iron artifacts, Sue pottery, and weapons provide invaluable insights into the burial practices of the mid-Kofun period. The tumulus is estimated to have been built during the early to mid-5th century.
== Excavations and Preservation == In 1964, an excavation led by [[Osaka University]] unearthed approximately 2,000 artifacts.<ref>"Crowdfunding Advances for 3D Digitalization of Important Artifacts," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' December 22, 2018.</ref> The site was designated as a National Historic Site in 1995 and incorporated into the unified designation of the "Furuichi Kofun Cluster" in 2001. The iron armor and other artifacts are stored at Osaka University and were designated as Important Cultural Properties of Japan in 2015.<ref>"Osaka City Art Museum Registered as Cultural Property," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' March 15, 2015.</ref>
Additionally, a 1964 color film documenting the excavation was rediscovered in 2009.<ref>"1964 Nonaka Kofun Excavation Film Found," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' December 24, 2012.</ref>

{{Infobox Kofun | name = Nonaka Kofun | native_name = 野中古墳 | location = {{ill|Fujiidera, Osaka|ja|藤井寺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|33|36.9|N|135|36|9.3|E|display=inline,title}} | region = Japan | type = {{ill|Square tumulus (Hōfun)|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} | length = 37m per side | built = 5th century | burial = Wooden coffin, wooden chest | artifacts = Armor, iron weapons | designated = National [[Historic Site of Japan|Historic Site]] (part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}) | map = Japan Osaka | map_caption = Location in Osaka Prefecture | image = {{image requested|cat=Fujiidera, Osaka}} }} '''Nonaka Kofun''' (野中古墳) is a mid-[[Kofun period]] Kofun ([[kofun]]) located in {{ill|Fujidera|ja|藤井寺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. A significant number of iron weapons, armor, and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器}} have been excavated from the site. The kofun is designated as a part of the [[UNESCO]] [[World Cultural Heritage]] "Ancient Kofun Clusters," specifically within the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}}. It is also a [[National Historic Site of Japan]].
== Overview == The Nonaka Kofun is part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} and is situated north of the rear circle of the {{ill|Makasuyama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳|1=Hakayama Kofun}}, leading to speculation that it may have served as a {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|subsidiary Kofun}} for the latter.<ref name="yomiuri140212">"Restoration Completed for Nonaka Kofun Armor," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' February 12, 2014.</ref>
The mound is a square-shaped tumulus (方墳) built in two tiers, with each side measuring 37 meters and a height of 4 meters. It features a surrounding moat, {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (roofing stones), cylindrical haniwa rows, and figurative haniwa, including helmets, "Kinugasa" lids, and horned helmets (衝角付冑). Numerous {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器}} were excavated from the mound's summit.<ref name="yomiuri140212"/>
Beneath the mound's summit, five sets of burial facilities were aligned in parallel, consisting of wooden coffins and wooden chests. Only the second wooden coffin is believed to have contained human remains, while the other four are interpreted as dedicated for grave goods. These facilities yielded a significant collection of grave goods, including: * 11 sets of iron armor ([[tankō]] 短甲) * 11 helmets (冑) * Components such as grass skirts and shoulder guards * Iron swords (鉄刀), iron spears (鉄剣), and a large number of iron arrowheads (鉄鏃).
The abundant iron artifacts, Sue pottery, and weapons provide invaluable insights into the burial practices of the mid-Kofun period. The tumulus is estimated to have been built during the early to mid-5th century.
== Excavations and Preservation == In 1964, an excavation led by [[Osaka University]] unearthed approximately 2,000 artifacts.<ref>"Crowdfunding Advances for 3D Digitalization of Important Artifacts," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' December 22, 2018.</ref> The site was designated as a National Historic Site in 1995 and incorporated into the unified designation of the "Furuichi Kofun Cluster" in 2001. The iron armor and other artifacts are stored at Osaka University and were designated as Important Cultural Properties of Japan in 2015.<ref>"Osaka City Art Museum Registered as Cultural Property," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' March 15, 2015.</ref>
Additionally, a 1964 color film documenting the excavation was rediscovered in 2009.<ref>"1964 Nonaka Kofun Excavation Film Found," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' December 24, 2012.</ref>

revision.comment: moving the kofun template to the bottom of each page
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Kofun | name = Nonaka Kofun | native_name = 野中古墳 | location = {{ill|Fujiidera, Osaka|ja|藤井寺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|33|36.9|N|135|36|9.3|E|display=inline,title}} | region = Japan | type = {{ill|Square tumulus (Hōfun)|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} | length = 37m per side | built = 5th century | burial = Wooden coffin, wooden chest | artifacts = Armor, iron weapons | designated = National [[Historic Site of Japan|Historic Site]] (part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}) | map = Japan Osaka | map_caption = Location in Osaka Prefecture | image = {{image requested|cat=Fujiidera, Osaka}} }} '''Nonaka Kofun''' (野中古墳) is a mid-[[Kofun period]] Kofun ([[kofun]]) located in {{ill|Fujidera|ja|藤井寺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, Japan. A significant number of iron weapons, armor, and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器}} have been excavated from the site. The kofun is designated as a part of the [[UNESCO]] [[World Cultural Heritage]] "Ancient Kofun Clusters," specifically within the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}}. It is also a [[National Historic Site of Japan]].
== Overview == The Nonaka Kofun is part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} and is situated north of the rear circle of the {{ill|Makasuyama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳|1=Hakayama Kofun}}, leading to speculation that it may have served as a {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|subsidiary Kofun}} for the latter.<ref name="yomiuri140212">"Restoration Completed for Nonaka Kofun Armor," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' February 12, 2014.</ref>
The mound is a square-shaped tumulus (方墳) built in two tiers, with each side measuring 37 meters and a height of 4 meters. It features a surrounding moat, {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (roofing stones), cylindrical haniwa rows, and figurative haniwa, including helmets, "Kinugasa" lids, and horned helmets (衝角付冑). Numerous {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器}} were excavated from the mound's summit.<ref name="yomiuri140212"/>
Beneath the mound's summit, five sets of burial facilities were aligned in parallel, consisting of wooden coffins and wooden chests. Only the second wooden coffin is believed to have contained human remains, while the other four are interpreted as dedicated for grave goods. These facilities yielded a significant collection of grave goods, including: * 11 sets of iron armor ([[tankō]] 短甲) * 11 helmets (冑) * Components such as grass skirts and shoulder guards * Iron swords (鉄刀), iron spears (鉄剣), and a large number of iron arrowheads (鉄鏃).
The abundant iron artifacts, Sue pottery, and weapons provide invaluable insights into the burial practices of the mid-Kofun period. The tumulus is estimated to have been built during the early to mid-5th century.
== Excavations and Preservation == In 1964, an excavation led by [[Osaka University]] unearthed approximately 2,000 artifacts.<ref>"Crowdfunding Advances for 3D Digitalization of Important Artifacts," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' December 22, 2018.</ref> The site was designated as a National Historic Site in 1995 and incorporated into the unified designation of the "Furuichi Kofun Cluster" in 2001. The iron armor and other artifacts are stored at Osaka University and were designated as Important Cultural Properties of Japan in 2015.<ref>"Osaka City Art Museum Registered as Cultural Property," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' March 15, 2015.</ref>
Additionally, a 1964 color film documenting the excavation was rediscovered in 2009.<ref>"1964 Nonaka Kofun Excavation Film Found," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' December 24, 2012.</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Literature == * ''Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia,'' Tokyo-dō Shuppan, 1989.
== Related Sites == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}} * {{ill|Nonaka Ariyama Kofun|ja|野中アリ山古墳}} * {{ill|Katombo-yama Kofun|ja|カトンボ山古墳}} - A baizuka featuring similar abundant burial goods, including imitation stone products and beads.
== External Links == * [http://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/10kakuka/34shakaikyoiku/03bunkazai/04isekishokai/13kaisetsu/k_nonaka.html Nonaka Kofun] (Habikino City Website) * [http://www.let.osaka-u.ac.jp/kouko/nonaka/ Nonaka Kofun] (Osaka University Archaeology Laboratory)
