{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Kofun | name = Nonaka Kofun | native_name = 野中古墳 | location = {{ill|Fujiidera, Osaka|ja|藤井寺市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|33|36.9|N|135|36|9.3|E|display=inline,title}} | region = Japan | type = {{ill|Square tumulus (Hōfun)|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=draft}} | length = 37m per side | built = 5th century | burial = Wooden coffin, wooden chest | burial goods = Armor, iron weapons | designation1 = National [[Historic Site of Japan|Historic Site]] (part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群|12=draft}}) | map = Japan Osaka | map_caption = location in Osaka Prefecture | image = {{image requested|cat=Fujiidera, Osaka}} }} '''Nonaka Kofun''' (野中古墳) is a mid-[[Kofun period]] Kofun ([[kofun]]) located in {{ill|Fujidera|ja|藤井寺市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft}}, Japan. A significant number of iron weapons, armor, and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}} have been excavated from the site. The kofun is designation1 as a part of the [[UNESCO]] [[World Historic Site]] "Ancient Kofun Clusters," specifically within the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群|12=draft}}. It is also a [[National Historic Site of Japan]].
== Overview == The Nonaka Kofun is part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群|12=draft}} and is situated north of the rear circle of the {{ill|Makasuyama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳|1=Hakayama Kofun|12=draft}}, leading to speculation that it may have served as a {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|subsidiary Kofun|12=draft}} for the latter.<ref name="yomiuri140212">"Restoration Completed for Nonaka Kofun Armor," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' February 12, 2014.</ref>
The mound is a square-shaped tumulus (方墳) built in two tiers, with each side measuring 37 meters and a height of 4 meters. It features a surrounding moat, {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|12=draft}} (roofing stones), cylindrical haniwa rows, and figurative haniwa, including helmets, "Kinugasa" lids, and horned helmets (衝角付冑). Numerous {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft}} were excavated from the mound's summit.<ref name="yomiuri140212"/>
Beneath the mound's summit, five sets of burial facilities were aligned in parallel, consisting of wooden coffins and wooden chests. Only the second wooden coffin is believed to have contained human remains, while the other four are interpreted as dedicated for grave goods. These facilities yielded a significant collection of grave goods, including: * 11 sets of iron armor ([[tankō]] 短甲) * 11 helmets (冑) * Components such as grass skirts and shoulder guards * Iron swords (鉄刀), iron spears (鉄剣), and a large number of iron arrowheads (鉄鏃).
The abundant iron burial goods, Sue pottery, and weapons provide invaluable insights into the burial practices of the mid-Kofun period. The tumulus is estimated to have been built during the early to mid-5th century.
== Excavations and Preservation == In 1964, an excavation led by [[Osaka University]] unearthed approximately 2,000 burial goods.<ref>"Crowdfunding Advances for 3D Digitalization of Important burial goods," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' December 22, 2018.</ref> The site was designation1 as a National Historic Site in 1995 and incorporated into the unified designation of the "Furuichi Kofun Cluster" in 2001. The iron armor and other burial goods are stored at Osaka University and were designation1 as [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] of Japan in 2015.<ref>"Osaka City Art Museum Registered as Cultural Property," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' March 15, 2015.</ref>
Additionally, a 1964 color film documenting the excavation was rediscovered in 2009.<ref>"1964 Nonaka Kofun Excavation Film Found," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' December 24, 2012.</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Literature == * ''Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia,'' Tokyo-dō Shuppan, 1989.
== Related Sites == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群|12=draft}} * {{ill|Nonaka Ariyama Kofun|ja|野中アリ山古墳|12=draft}} * {{ill|Katombo-yama Kofun|ja|カトンボ山古墳|12=draft}} - A baizuka featuring similar abundant burial goods, including imitation stone products and beads.
== External Links == * [http://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/10kakuka/34shakaikyoiku/03bunkazai/04isekishokai/13kaisetsu/k_nonaka.html Nonaka Kofun] (Habikino City Website) * [http://www.let.osaka-u.ac.jp/kouko/nonaka/ Nonaka Kofun] (Osaka University Archaeology Laboratory)

== Overview == The Nonaka Kofun is part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群|12=draft}} and is situated north of the rear circle of the {{ill|Makasuyama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳|1=Hakayama Kofun|12=draft}}, leading to speculation that it may have served as a {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|subsidiary Kofun|12=draft}} for the latter.<ref name="yomiuri140212">"Restoration Completed for Nonaka Kofun Armor," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' February 12, 2014.</ref>
The mound is a square-shaped tumulus (方墳) built in two tiers, with each side measuring 37 meters and a height of 4 meters. It features a surrounding moat, {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|12=draft}} (roofing stones), cylindrical haniwa rows, and figurative haniwa, including helmets, "Kinugasa" lids, and horned helmets (衝角付冑). Numerous {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft}} were excavated from the mound's summit.<ref name="yomiuri140212"/>
Beneath the mound's summit, five sets of burial facilities were aligned in parallel, consisting of wooden coffins and wooden chests. Only the second wooden coffin is believed to have contained human remains, while the other four are interpreted as dedicated for grave goods. These facilities yielded a significant collection of grave goods, including: * 11 sets of iron armor ([[tankō]] 短甲) * 11 helmets (冑) * Components such as grass skirts and shoulder guards * Iron swords (鉄刀), iron spears (鉄剣), and a large number of iron arrowheads (鉄鏃).
The abundant iron burial goods, Sue pottery, and weapons provide invaluable insights into the burial practices of the mid-Kofun period. The tumulus is estimated to have been built during the early to mid-5th century.
== Excavations and Preservation == In 1964, an excavation led by [[Osaka University]] unearthed approximately 2,000 burial goods.<ref>"Crowdfunding Advances for 3D Digitalization of Important burial goods," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' December 22, 2018.</ref> The site was designation1 as a National Historic Site in 1995 and incorporated into the unified designation of the "Furuichi Kofun Cluster" in 2001. The iron armor and other burial goods are stored at Osaka University and were designation1 as [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] of Japan in 2015.<ref>"Osaka City Art Museum Registered as Cultural Property," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' March 15, 2015.</ref>
Additionally, a 1964 color film documenting the excavation was rediscovered in 2009.<ref>"1964 Nonaka Kofun Excavation Film Found," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' December 24, 2012.</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Literature == * ''Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia,'' Tokyo-dō Shuppan, 1989.
== Related Sites == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群|12=draft}} * {{ill|Nonaka Ariyama Kofun|ja|野中アリ山古墳|12=draft}} * {{ill|Katombo-yama Kofun|ja|カトンボ山古墳|12=draft}} - A baizuka featuring similar abundant burial goods, including imitation stone products and beads.

The kofun is designation1 as a part of the [[UNESCO]] [[World Historic Site]] "Ancient Kofun Clusters," specifically within the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群|12=draft}}. revision.comment: Bot: single‑shot reformat + ill→|12=draft + draft categories + iws + DEFAULTSORT + move HTML comments + meta‑cat check
25 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:野中古墳 up to rev 103989553
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称= 野中古墳 |画像=[[File:Nonakakohun.jpg|300px]] |所属=[[古市古墳群]] |所在地=大阪府藤井寺市野中 |位置={{Coord|34|33|36.9|N|135|36|9.3|E|region:JP-27}} |形状=方墳 |規模=一辺37m |築造年代=5世紀 |埋葬施設=木棺 木櫃 |被葬者= |出土品=甲冑 鉄製武器 |指定文化財=国の[[史跡]] |特記事項= }} '''野中古墳'''（のなかこふん）は、[[大阪府]][[藤井寺市]]野中3丁目に所在する[[古墳時代]]中期の古墳（[[方墳]]）である。鉄製の武器、武具、[[須恵器]]など大量の遺物が出土した。[[文化遺産 (世界遺産)|世界文化遺産]]の「[[古市古墳群]]」の一部として国の[[史跡]]に指定されている。
== 概要== [[古市古墳群]]に属し、[[墓山古墳]]の後円部北側に位置しており同古墳の[[陪塚]]とも推定されている<ref name="yomiuri140212">『読売新聞』2014年2月12日朝刊文化面19頁「野中古墳 甲冑修復終える」（読売新聞東京本社）</ref>。墳丘は2段に築成された方墳であり、一辺37m{{R|yomiuri140212}}、高さ4m、周濠があり、[[葺石]]、[[円筒埴輪]]列、蓋（きぬがさ）、[[衝角付冑]]、囲形などの[[形象埴輪]]が存在し、墳頂部からは大量の[[須恵器]]、[[土師器]]が出土している{{R|yomiuri140212}}。墳頂部地下には組合式[[木棺]]と木櫃からなる内部主体が5列に並列して埋納されており、人体埋葬が考えられるのは第2列の木棺のみで、他の4列はすべて、副葬品埋納施設と推定される。それらの施設からは[[短甲]]11領、[[冑]]11箇のほか、草摺、肩甲などの鉄製武具、[[直刀|鉄刀]]、[[剣|鉄剣]]、[[鉄鏃]]などの大量の武器が出土している。大量の鉄製品、須恵質土器、武器・武具の副葬形態など中期古墳の研究の上で欠くことのできない内容の古墳である。5世紀前半から中葉頃の築造と考えられている。
1964年、[[大阪大学]]が実施した発掘調査により遺物約2000点が出土した<ref>『読売新聞』2018年12月22日朝刊大阪2面32頁「重文遺物3D化 前進 阪大計測計画 ネット募金 目標150万円達成＝大阪」（読売新聞大阪本社）</ref>。1995年に国の史跡に指定される。2001年、既指定の他の古墳と統合指定され、国の史跡「古市古墳群」の一部となっている。[[甲冑]]などの出土品は大阪大学に所蔵されており、2015年に国の重要文化財に指定されている<ref>『読売新聞』2015年3月15日朝刊市内面33頁「大阪市立美術館 国登録文化財に 審議会答申＝大阪」（読売新聞大阪本社）</ref>。なお、1964年に本遺跡の発掘調査を撮影したカラー映像が2009年に発見された<ref>『読売新聞』2012年12月24日朝刊河内面23頁「「百舌鳥・古市」野中古墳 48年前の発掘 鮮明映像＝大阪」（読売新聞大阪本社）</ref>。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 文献 == * 『日本古墳大辞典』、株式会社東京堂出版、1989年。
== 関連項目 == * [[古市古墳群]] * [[野中アリ山古墳]] * [[カトンボ山古墳]] - 野中古墳と同様に多量の滑石製模造品・臼玉が埋納された陪塚。
== 外部リンク == *[http://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/10kakuka/34shakaikyoiku/03bunkazai/04isekishokai/13kaisetsu/k_nonaka.html 野中古墳（のなかこふん）]（羽曳野市ウェブサイト） *[http://www.let.osaka-u.ac.jp/kouko/nonaka/ 野中古墳]（大阪大学考古学研究室）
{{Normdaten}} {{デフォルトソート:のなかこふん}} [[Category:方墳]] [[Category:大阪府にある国指定の史跡]] [[Category:大阪府の古墳]] [[Category:藤井寺市の歴史]] [[Category:古市古墳群]]

revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称= 野中古墳 |画像=[[File:Nonakakohun.jpg|300px]] |所属=[[古市古墳群]] |所在地=大阪府藤井寺市野中 |位置={{Coord|34|33|36.9|N|135|36|9.3|E|region:JP-27}} |形状=方墳 |規模=一辺37m |築造年代=5世紀 |埋葬施設=木棺 木櫃 |被葬者= |出土品=甲冑 鉄製武器 |指定文化財=国の[[史跡]] |特記事項= }} '''野中古墳'''（のなかこふん）は、[[大阪府]][[藤井寺市]]野中3丁目に所在する[[古墳時代]]中期の古墳（[[方墳]]）である。鉄製の武器、武具、[[須恵器]]など大量の遺物が出土した。[[文化遺産 (世界遺産)|世界文化遺産]]の「[[古市古墳群]]」の一部として国の[[史跡]]に指定されている。
== 概要== [[古市古墳群]]に属し、[[墓山古墳]]の後円部北側に位置しており同古墳の[[陪塚]]とも推定されている<ref name="yomiuri140212">『読売新聞』2014年2月12日朝刊文化面19頁「野中古墳 甲冑修復終える」（読売新聞東京本社）</ref>。墳丘は2段に築成された方墳であり、一辺37m{{R|yomiuri140212}}、高さ4m、周濠があり、[[葺石]]、[[円筒埴輪]]列、蓋（きぬがさ）、[[衝角付冑]]、囲形などの[[形象埴輪]]が存在し、墳頂部からは大量の[[須恵器]]、[[土師器]]が出土している{{R|yomiuri140212}}。墳頂部地下には組合式[[木棺]]と木櫃からなる内部主体が5列に並列して埋納されており、人体埋葬が考えられるのは第2列の木棺のみで、他の4列はすべて、副葬品埋納施設と推定される。それらの施設からは[[短甲]]11領、[[冑]]11箇のほか、草摺、肩甲などの鉄製武具、[[直刀|鉄刀]]、[[剣|鉄剣]]、[[鉄鏃]]などの大量の武器が出土している。大量の鉄製品、須恵質土器、武器・武具の副葬形態など中期古墳の研究の上で欠くことのできない内容の古墳である。5世紀前半から中葉頃の築造と考えられている。
1964年、[[大阪大学]]が実施した発掘調査により遺物約2000点が出土した<ref>『読売新聞』2018年12月22日朝刊大阪2面32頁「重文遺物3D化 前進 阪大計測計画 ネット募金 目標150万円達成＝大阪」（読売新聞大阪本社）</ref>。1995年に国の史跡に指定される。2001年、既指定の他の古墳と統合指定され、国の史跡「古市古墳群」の一部となっている。[[甲冑]]などの出土品は大阪大学に所蔵されており、2015年に国の重要文化財に指定されている<ref>『読売新聞』2015年3月15日朝刊市内面33頁「大阪市立美術館 国登録文化財に 審議会答申＝大阪」（読売新聞大阪本社）</ref>。なお、1964年に本遺跡の発掘調査を撮影したカラー映像が2009年に発見された<ref>『読売新聞』2012年12月24日朝刊河内面23頁「「百舌鳥・古市」野中古墳 48年前の発掘 鮮明映像＝大阪」（読売新聞大阪本社）</ref>。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 文献 == * 『日本古墳大辞典』、株式会社東京堂出版、1989年。
== 関連項目 == * [[古市古墳群]] * [[野中アリ山古墳]] * [[カトンボ山古墳]] - 野中古墳と同様に多量の滑石製模造品・臼玉が埋納された陪塚。
== 外部リンク == *[http://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/10kakuka/34shakaikyoiku/03bunkazai/04isekishokai/13kaisetsu/k_nonaka.html 野中古墳（のなかこふん）]（羽曳野市ウェブサイト） *[http://www.let.osaka-u.ac.jp/kouko/nonaka/ 野中古墳]（大阪大学考古学研究室）
{{Normdaten}} {{デフォルトソート:のなかこふん}} [[Category:方墳]] [[Category:大阪府にある国指定の史跡]] [[Category:大阪府の古墳]] [[Category:藤井寺市の歴史]] [[Category:古市古墳群]]

'''Nonaka Kofun''' (野中古墳) is a mid-[[Kofun period]] Kofun ([[kofun]]) located in [[:en:Fujiidera|Fujidera]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. A significant number of iron weapons, armor, and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] have been excavated from the site. The kofun is designation1 as a part of the [[:en:UNESCO|UNESCO]] [[World Historic Site]] "Ancient Kofun Clusters," specifically within the [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Cluster]]. It is also a [[National Historic Site of Japan]].

== Overview ==
The Nonaka Kofun is part of the [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] and is situated north of the rear circle of the {{ill|Makasuyama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳|1=Hakayama Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makasuyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakayama Kofun|qq=}}, leading to speculation that it may have served as a {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|subsidiary Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baizuka|qq=}} for the latter.<ref name="yomiuri140212">"Restoration Completed for Nonaka Kofun Armor," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' February 12, 2014.</ref> 

The mound is a square-shaped tumulus (方墳) built in two tiers, with each side measuring 37 meters and a height of 4 meters. It features a surrounding moat, [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] (roofing stones), cylindrical haniwa rows, and figurative haniwa, including helmets, "Kinugasa" lids, and horned helmets (衝角付冑). Numerous [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] were excavated from the mound's summit.<ref name="yomiuri140212"/>

Beneath the mound's summit, five sets of burial facilities were aligned in parallel, consisting of wooden coffins and wooden chests. Only the second wooden coffin is believed to have contained human remains, while the other four are interpreted as dedicated for grave goods. These facilities yielded a significant collection of grave goods, including:
* 11 sets of iron armor ([[tankō]] 短甲)
* 11 helmets (冑)
* Components such as grass skirts and shoulder guards
* Iron swords (鉄刀), iron spears (鉄剣), and a large number of iron arrowheads (鉄鏃).

The abundant iron burial goods, Sue pottery, and weapons provide invaluable insights into the burial practices of the mid-Kofun period. The tumulus is estimated to have been built during the early to mid-5th century.

== Excavations and Preservation ==
In 1964, an excavation led by [[:en:Osaka University|Osaka University]] unearthed approximately 2,000 burial goods.<ref>"Crowdfunding Advances for 3D Digitalization of Important burial goods," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' December 22, 2018.</ref> The site was designation1 as a National Historic Site in 1995 and incorporated into the unified designation of the "Furuichi Kofun Cluster" in 2001. The iron armor and other burial goods are stored at Osaka University and were designation1 as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] of Japan in 2015.<ref>"Osaka City Art Museum Registered as Cultural Property," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' March 15, 2015.</ref> 

Additionally, a 1964 color film documenting the excavation was rediscovered in 2009.<ref>"1964 Nonaka Kofun Excavation Film Found," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' December 24, 2012.</ref>

== References ==
{{Reflist}}

== Literature ==
* ''Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia,'' Tokyo-dō Shuppan, 1989.

== Related Sites ==
* [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Cluster]]
* {{ill|Nonaka Ariyama Kofun|ja|野中アリ山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nonaka Ariyama Kofun|qq=}}
* {{ill|Katomboyama Kofun|lt=Katomboyama Kofun|lt=Katombo-yama Kofun|ja|カトンボ山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katombo-yama Kofun|qq=}} - A baizuka featuring similar abundant burial goods, including imitation stone products and beads.

== External Links ==
* [http://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/10kakuka/34shakaikyoiku/03bunkazai/04isekishokai/13kaisetsu/k_nonaka.html Nonaka Kofun] (Habikino City Website)
* [http://www.let.osaka-u.ac.jp/kouko/nonaka/ Nonaka Kofun] (Osaka University Archaeology Laboratory)

A significant number of iron weapons, armor, and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] have been excavated from the site. The kofun is designation1 as a part of the [[:en:UNESCO|UNESCO]] [[World Historic Site]] "Ancient Kofun Clusters," specifically within the [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Cluster]]. It is also a [[National Historic Site of Japan]].

== Overview ==
The Nonaka Kofun is part of the [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] and is situated north of the rear circle of the {{ill|Makasuyama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳|1=Hakayama Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makasuyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakayama Kofun|qq=}}, leading to speculation that it may have served as a {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|subsidiary Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baizuka|qq=}} for the latter.<ref name="yomiuri140212">"Restoration Completed for Nonaka Kofun Armor," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' February 12, 2014.</ref> 

The mound is a square-shaped tumulus (方墳) built in two tiers, with each side measuring 37 meters and a height of 4 meters. Beneath the mound's summit, five sets of burial facilities were aligned in parallel, consisting of wooden coffins and wooden chests. Only the second wooden coffin is believed to have contained human remains, while the other four are interpreted as dedicated for grave goods. These facilities yielded a significant collection of grave goods, including:

* 11 helmets (冑)

== Excavations and Preservation ==
In 1964, an excavation led by [[:en:Osaka University|Osaka University]] unearthed approximately 2,000 burial goods.<ref>"Crowdfunding Advances for 3D Digitalization of Important burial goods," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' December 22, 2018.</ref> The site was designation1 as a National Historic Site in 1995 and incorporated into the unified designation of the "Furuichi Kofun Cluster" in 2001. '''Nonaka Kofun''' (野中古墳) is a mid-[[Kofun period]] Kofun ([[kofun]]) located in [[:en:Fujiidera|Fujidera]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. A significant number of iron weapons, armor, and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] have been excavated from the site. The kofun is designation1 as a part of the [[:en:UNESCO|UNESCO]] [[World Historic Site]] "Ancient Kofun Clusters," specifically within the [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Cluster]]. It is also a [[National Historic Site of Japan]].

== Overview ==
The Nonaka Kofun is part of the [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] and is situated north of the rear circle of the {{ill|Makasuyama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳|1=Hakayama Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makasuyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakayama Kofun|qq=}}, leading to speculation that it may have served as a {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|subsidiary Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baizuka|qq=}} for the latter.<ref name="yomiuri140212">"Restoration Completed for Nonaka Kofun Armor," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' February 12, 2014.</ref> 

The mound is a square-shaped tumulus (方墳) built in two tiers, with each side measuring 37 meters and a height of 4 meters. It features a surrounding moat, [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] (roofing stones), cylindrical haniwa rows, and figurative haniwa, including helmets, "Kinugasa" lids, and horned helmets (衝角付冑). Numerous [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] were excavated from the mound's summit.<ref name="yomiuri140212"/>

Beneath the mound's summit, five sets of burial facilities were aligned in parallel, consisting of wooden coffins and wooden chests. Only the second wooden coffin is believed to have contained human remains, while the other four are interpreted as dedicated for grave goods. These facilities yielded a significant collection of grave goods, including:
* 11 sets of iron armor ([[tankō]] 短甲)
* 11 helmets (冑)
* Components such as grass skirts and shoulder guards
* Iron swords (鉄刀), iron spears (鉄剣), and a large number of iron arrowheads (鉄鏃).

The abundant iron burial goods, Sue pottery, and weapons provide invaluable insights into the burial practices of the mid-Kofun period. The tumulus is estimated to have been built during the early to mid-5th century.

== Excavations and Preservation ==
In 1964, an excavation led by [[:en:Osaka University|Osaka University]] unearthed approximately 2,000 burial goods.<ref>"Crowdfunding Advances for 3D Digitalization of Important burial goods," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' December 22, 2018.</ref> The site was designation1 as a National Historic Site in 1995 and incorporated into the unified designation of the "Furuichi Kofun Cluster" in 2001. The iron armor and other burial goods are stored at Osaka University and were designation1 as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] of Japan in 2015.<ref>"Osaka City Art Museum Registered as Cultural Property," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' March 15, 2015.</ref> 

Additionally, a 1964 color film documenting the excavation was rediscovered in 2009.<ref>"1964 Nonaka Kofun Excavation Film Found," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' December 24, 2012.</ref>

| name        = Nonaka Kofun

| length      = 37m per side
| built       = 5th century

| burial goods   = Armor, iron weapons
| designation1  = National [[Historic Site of Japan|Historic Site]] (part of the [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Group]])
| map         = Japan Osaka
| map_caption = location in Osaka Prefecture

'''Nonaka Kofun''' (野中古墳) is a mid-[[Kofun period]] Kofun ([[kofun]]) located in [[:en:Fujiidera|Fujidera]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. A significant number of iron weapons, armor, and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] have been excavated from the site. The kofun is designation1 as a part of the [[:en:UNESCO|UNESCO]] [[World Historic Site]] "Ancient Kofun Clusters," specifically within the [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Cluster]]. It is also a [[National Historic Site of Japan]].

== Overview ==
The Nonaka Kofun is part of the [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] and is situated north of the rear circle of the {{ill|Makasuyama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳|1=Hakayama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makasuyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakayama Kofun|qq=}}, leading to speculation that it may have served as a {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|subsidiary Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baizuka|qq=}} for the latter.<ref name="yomiuri140212">"Restoration Completed for Nonaka Kofun Armor," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' February 12, 2014.</ref> 

The mound is a square-shaped tumulus (方墳) built in two tiers, with each side measuring 37 meters and a height of 4 meters. It features a surrounding moat, [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] (roofing stones), cylindrical haniwa rows, and figurative haniwa, including helmets, "Kinugasa" lids, and horned helmets (衝角付冑). Numerous [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] were excavated from the mound's summit.<ref name="yomiuri140212"/>

Beneath the mound's summit, five sets of burial facilities were aligned in parallel, consisting of wooden coffins and wooden chests. Only the second wooden coffin is believed to have contained human remains, while the other four are interpreted as dedicated for grave goods. These facilities yielded a significant collection of grave goods, including:
* 11 sets of iron armor ([[tankō]] 短甲)
* 11 helmets (冑)
* Components such as grass skirts and shoulder guards
* Iron swords (鉄刀), iron spears (鉄剣), and a large number of iron arrowheads (鉄鏃).

The abundant iron burial goods, Sue pottery, and weapons provide invaluable insights into the burial practices of the mid-Kofun period. The tumulus is estimated to have been built during the early to mid-5th century.

== Excavations and Preservation ==
In 1964, an excavation led by [[:en:Osaka University|Osaka University]] unearthed approximately 2,000 burial goods.<ref>"Crowdfunding Advances for 3D Digitalization of Important burial goods," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' December 22, 2018.</ref> The site was designation1 as a National Historic Site in 1995 and incorporated into the unified designation of the "Furuichi Kofun Cluster" in 2001. The iron armor and other burial goods are stored at Osaka University and were designation1 as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] of Japan in 2015.<ref>"Osaka City Art Museum Registered as Cultural Property," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' March 15, 2015.</ref> 

Additionally, a 1964 color film documenting the excavation was rediscovered in 2009.<ref>"1964 Nonaka Kofun Excavation Film Found," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' December 24, 2012.</ref>

'''Nonaka Kofun''' (野中古墳) is a mid-[[Kofun period]] Kofun ([[kofun]]) located in [[:en:Fujiidera|Fujidera]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. A significant number of iron weapons, armor, and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] have been excavated from the site. The kofun is designation1 as a part of the [[:en:UNESCO|UNESCO]] [[World Historic Site]] "Ancient Kofun Clusters," specifically within the [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Cluster]]. It is also a [[National Historic Site of Japan]].

== Overview ==
The Nonaka Kofun is part of the [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] and is situated north of the rear circle of the {{ill|Makasuyama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳|1=Hakayama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makasuyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakayama Kofun|qq=}}, leading to speculation that it may have served as a {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|subsidiary Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baizuka|qq=}} for the latter.<ref name="yomiuri140212">"Restoration Completed for Nonaka Kofun Armor," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' February 12, 2014.</ref> 

The mound is a square-shaped tumulus (方墳) built in two tiers, with each side measuring 37 meters and a height of 4 meters. It features a surrounding moat, [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] (roofing stones), cylindrical haniwa rows, and figurative haniwa, including helmets, "Kinugasa" lids, and horned helmets (衝角付冑). Numerous [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] were excavated from the mound's summit.<ref name="yomiuri140212"/>

Beneath the mound's summit, five sets of burial facilities were aligned in parallel, consisting of wooden coffins and wooden chests. Only the second wooden coffin is believed to have contained human remains, while the other four are interpreted as dedicated for grave goods. These facilities yielded a significant collection of grave goods, including:
* 11 sets of iron armor ([[tankō]] 短甲)
* 11 helmets (冑)
* Components such as grass skirts and shoulder guards
* Iron swords (鉄刀), iron spears (鉄剣), and a large number of iron arrowheads (鉄鏃).

The abundant iron burial goods, Sue pottery, and weapons provide invaluable insights into the burial practices of the mid-Kofun period. The tumulus is estimated to have been built during the early to mid-5th century.

== Excavations and Preservation ==
In 1964, an excavation led by [[:en:Osaka University|Osaka University]] unearthed approximately 2,000 burial goods.<ref>"Crowdfunding Advances for 3D Digitalization of Important burial goods," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' December 22, 2018.</ref> The site was designation1 as a National Historic Site in 1995 and incorporated into the unified designation of the "Furuichi Kofun Cluster" in 2001. The iron armor and other burial goods are stored at Osaka University and were designation1 as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] of Japan in 2015.<ref>"Osaka City Art Museum Registered as Cultural Property," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' March 15, 2015.</ref> 

Additionally, a 1964 color film documenting the excavation was rediscovered in 2009.<ref>"1964 Nonaka Kofun Excavation Film Found," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' December 24, 2012.</ref>

It is also a [[National Historic Site of Japan]].

== Overview ==
The Nonaka Kofun is part of the [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] and is situated north of the rear circle of the {{ill|Makasuyama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳|1=Hakayama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makasuyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakayama Kofun|qq=}}, leading to speculation that it may have served as a {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|subsidiary Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baizuka|qq=}} for the latter.<ref name="yomiuri140212">"Restoration Completed for Nonaka Kofun Armor," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' February 12, 2014.</ref> 

The mound is a square-shaped tumulus (方墳) built in two tiers, with each side measuring 37 meters and a height of 4 meters. It features a surrounding moat, [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] (roofing stones), cylindrical haniwa rows, and figurative haniwa, including helmets, "Kinugasa" lids, and horned helmets (衝角付冑). Numerous [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] were excavated from the mound's summit.<ref name="yomiuri140212"/>

Beneath the mound's summit, five sets of burial facilities were aligned in parallel, consisting of wooden coffins and wooden chests. Only the second wooden coffin is believed to have contained human remains, while the other four are interpreted as dedicated for grave goods. These facilities yielded a significant collection of grave goods, including:
* 11 sets of iron armor ([[tankō]] 短甲)
* 11 helmets (冑)
* Components such as grass skirts and shoulder guards
* Iron swords (鉄刀), iron spears (鉄剣), and a large number of iron arrowheads (鉄鏃).

The abundant iron burial goods, Sue pottery, and weapons provide invaluable insights into the burial practices of the mid-Kofun period. The tumulus is estimated to have been built during the early to mid-5th century.

== Excavations and Preservation ==
In 1964, an excavation led by [[:en:Osaka University|Osaka University]] unearthed approximately 2,000 burial goods.<ref>"Crowdfunding Advances for 3D Digitalization of Important burial goods," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' December 22, 2018.</ref> The site was designation1 as a National Historic Site in 1995 and incorporated into the unified designation of the "Furuichi Kofun Cluster" in 2001. The iron armor and other burial goods are stored at Osaka University and were designation1 as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] of Japan in 2015.<ref>"Osaka City Art Museum Registered as Cultural Property," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' March 15, 2015.</ref> 

Additionally, a 1964 color film documenting the excavation was rediscovered in 2009.<ref>"1964 Nonaka Kofun Excavation Film Found," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' December 24, 2012.</ref>

'''Nonaka Kofun''' (野中古墳) is a mid-[[Kofun period]] Kofun ([[kofun]]) located in [[:en:Fujiidera|Fujidera]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. A significant number of iron weapons, armor, and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] have been excavated from the site. The kofun is designation1 as a part of the [[:en:UNESCO|UNESCO]] [[World Historic Site]] "Ancient Kofun Clusters," specifically within the [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Cluster]]. It is also a [[National Historic Site of Japan]].

== Overview ==
The Nonaka Kofun is part of the [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] and is situated north of the rear circle of the {{ill|Makasuyama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳|1=Hakayama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makasuyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakayama Kofun|qq=}}, leading to speculation that it may have served as a {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|subsidiary Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baizuka|qq=}} for the latter.<ref name="yomiuri140212">"Restoration Completed for Nonaka Kofun Armor," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' February 12, 2014.</ref> 

The mound is a square-shaped tumulus (方墳) built in two tiers, with each side measuring 37 meters and a height of 4 meters. It features a surrounding moat, [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] (roofing stones), cylindrical haniwa rows, and figurative haniwa, including helmets, "Kinugasa" lids, and horned helmets (衝角付冑). Numerous [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] were excavated from the mound's summit.<ref name="yomiuri140212"/>

Beneath the mound's summit, five sets of burial facilities were aligned in parallel, consisting of wooden coffins and wooden chests. Only the second wooden coffin is believed to have contained human remains, while the other four are interpreted as dedicated for grave goods. These facilities yielded a significant collection of grave goods, including:
* 11 sets of iron armor ([[tankō]] 短甲)
* 11 helmets (冑)
* Components such as grass skirts and shoulder guards
* Iron swords (鉄刀), iron spears (鉄剣), and a large number of iron arrowheads (鉄鏃).

The abundant iron burial goods, Sue pottery, and weapons provide invaluable insights into the burial practices of the mid-Kofun period. The tumulus is estimated to have been built during the early to mid-5th century.

In 1964, an excavation led by [[:en:Osaka University|Osaka University]] unearthed approximately 2,000 burial goods.<ref>"Crowdfunding Advances for 3D Digitalization of Important burial goods," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' December 22, 2018.</ref> The site was designation1 as a National Historic Site in 1995 and incorporated into the unified designation of the "Furuichi Kofun Cluster" in 2001. | name        = Nonaka Kofun

| length      = 37m per side

| designation1  = National [[Historic Site of Japan|Historic Site]] (part of the [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Group]])
| map         = Japan Osaka
| map_caption = location in Osaka Prefecture

'''Nonaka Kofun''' (野中古墳) is a mid-[[Kofun period]] Kofun ([[kofun]]) located in [[:en:Fujiidera|Fujidera]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. A significant number of iron weapons, armor, and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] have been excavated from the site. The kofun is designation1 as a part of the [[:en:UNESCO|UNESCO]] [[World Historic Site]] "Ancient Kofun Clusters," specifically within the [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Cluster]]. It is also a [[National Historic Site of Japan]].

== Overview ==
The Nonaka Kofun is part of the [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Cluster]] and is situated north of the rear circle of the {{ill|Makasuyama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳|1=Hakayama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makasuyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakayama Kofun|qq=}}, leading to speculation that it may have served as a {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|subsidiary Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baizuka|qq=}} for the latter.<ref name="yomiuri140212">"Restoration Completed for Nonaka Kofun Armor," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' February 12, 2014.</ref> 

The mound is a square-shaped tumulus (方墳) built in two tiers, with each side measuring 37 meters and a height of 4 meters. It features a surrounding moat, [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] (roofing stones), cylindrical haniwa rows, and figurative haniwa, including helmets, "Kinugasa" lids, and horned helmets (衝角付冑). Numerous [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] were excavated from the mound's summit.<ref name="yomiuri140212"/>

Beneath the mound's summit, five sets of burial facilities were aligned in parallel, consisting of wooden coffins and wooden chests. Only the second wooden coffin is believed to have contained human remains, while the other four are interpreted as dedicated for grave goods. These facilities yielded a significant collection of grave goods, including:
* 11 sets of iron armor ([[tankō]] 短甲)
* 11 helmets (冑)
* Components such as grass skirts and shoulder guards
* Iron swords (鉄刀), iron spears (鉄剣), and a large number of iron arrowheads (鉄鏃).

The abundant iron burial goods, Sue pottery, and weapons provide invaluable insights into the burial practices of the mid-Kofun period. The tumulus is estimated to have been built during the early to mid-5th century.

== Excavations and Preservation ==
In 1964, an excavation led by [[:en:Osaka University|Osaka University]] unearthed approximately 2,000 burial goods.<ref>"Crowdfunding Advances for 3D Digitalization of Important burial goods," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' December 22, 2018.</ref> The site was designation1 as a National Historic Site in 1995 and incorporated into the unified designation of the "Furuichi Kofun Cluster" in 2001. The iron armor and other burial goods are stored at Osaka University and were designation1 as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] of Japan in 2015.<ref>"Osaka City Art Museum Registered as Cultural Property," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' March 15, 2015.</ref> 

Additionally, a 1964 color film documenting the excavation was rediscovered in 2009.<ref>"1964 Nonaka Kofun Excavation Film Found," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' December 24, 2012.</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:野中古墳 (藤井寺市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:野中古墳 (藤井寺市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:野中古墳 (藤井寺市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11645001]] Wikidata name: Nonaka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:35, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Nonaka Kofun | native_name = 野中古墳 | location = {{ill|Fujiidera|WD=Q634011}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.56025|N|135.60258333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | region = Japan | type = {{ill|Square tumulus (Hōfun)|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square tumulus (Hōfun)|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | length = 37m per side | built = 5th century | burial = Wooden coffin, wooden chest | burial goods = Armor, iron weapons | designation1 = National [[Historic Site of Japan|Historic Site]] (part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|en|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|WD=Q4250076|lt=Furuichi Kofun Group}}) | map = Japan Osaka | map_caption = location in Osaka Prefecture | image = {{image requested|cat=Fujiidera, Osaka}} | architectural_styles= {{ill|square kofun|WD=Q11504353}} | part_of= {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|WD=Q4250076}} }} '''Nonaka Kofun''' (野中古墳) is a mid-[[Kofun period]] Kofun ([[kofun]]) located in {{ill|Fujiidera|en|Fujiidera|WD=Q634011|lt=Fujidera}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}, Japan. A significant number of iron weapons, armor, and {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} have been excavated from the site. The kofun is designation1 as a part of the {{ill|UNESCO|en|UNESCO|WD=Q7809|lt=UNESCO}} [[World Historic Site]] "Ancient Kofun Clusters," specifically within the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|en|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|WD=Q4250076|lt=Furuichi Kofun Cluster}}. It is also a [[National Historic Site of Japan]].
== Overview == The Nonaka Kofun is part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|en|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|WD=Q4250076|lt=Furuichi Kofun Cluster}} and is situated north of the rear circle of the {{ill|Makasuyama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳|1=Hakayama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makasuyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakayama Kofun|qq=}}, leading to speculation that it may have served as a {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|subsidiary Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baizuka|qq=}} for the latter.<ref name="yomiuri140212">"Restoration Completed for Nonaka Kofun Armor," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' February 12, 2014.</ref>
The mound is a square-shaped tumulus (方墳) built in two tiers, with each side measuring 37 meters and a height of 4 meters. It features a surrounding moat, {{ill|Fukiishi|en|Fukiishi|WD=Q11621213|lt=fukiishi}} (roofing stones), cylindrical haniwa rows, and figurative haniwa, including helmets, "Kinugasa" lids, and horned helmets (衝角付冑). Numerous {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery}} were excavated from the mound's summit.<ref name="yomiuri140212"/>
Beneath the mound's summit, five sets of burial facilities were aligned in parallel, consisting of wooden coffins and wooden chests. Only the second wooden coffin is believed to have contained human remains, while the other four are interpreted as dedicated for grave goods. These facilities yielded a significant collection of grave goods, including: * 11 sets of iron armor ([[tankō]] 短甲) * 11 helmets (冑) * Components such as grass skirts and shoulder guards * Iron swords (鉄刀), iron spears (鉄剣), and a large number of iron arrowheads (鉄鏃).
The abundant iron burial goods, Sue pottery, and weapons provide invaluable insights into the burial practices of the mid-Kofun period. The tumulus is estimated to have been built during the early to mid-5th century.
== Excavations and Preservation == In 1964, an excavation led by [[:en:Osaka University|Osaka University]] unearthed approximately 2,000 burial goods.<ref>"Crowdfunding Advances for 3D Digitalization of Important burial goods," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' December 22, 2018.</ref> The site was designation1 as a National Historic Site in 1995 and incorporated into the unified designation of the "Furuichi Kofun Cluster" in 2001. The iron armor and other burial goods are stored at Osaka University and were designation1 as {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property}} of Japan in 2015.<ref>"Osaka City Art Museum Registered as Cultural Property," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' March 15, 2015.</ref>
Additionally, a 1964 color film documenting the excavation was rediscovered in 2009.<ref>"1964 Nonaka Kofun Excavation Film Found," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' December 24, 2012.</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Literature == * ''Japanese Kofun Encyclopedia,'' Tokyo-dō Shuppan, 1989.
== Related Sites == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|en|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|WD=Q4250076|lt=Furuichi Kofun Cluster}} * {{ill|Nonaka Ariyama Kofun|ja|野中アリ山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nonaka Ariyama Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Katomboyama Kofun|lt=Katomboyama Kofun|lt=Katombo-yama Kofun|ja|カトンボ山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katombo-yama Kofun|qq=}} - A baizuka featuring similar abundant burial goods, including imitation stone products and beads.
== External Links == * [http://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/10kakuka/34shakaikyoiku/03bunkazai/04isekishokai/13kaisetsu/k_nonaka.html Nonaka Kofun] (Habikino City Website) * [http://www.let.osaka-u.ac.jp/kouko/nonaka/ Nonaka Kofun] (Osaka University Archaeology Laboratory)
{{Japanese-history-stub}}
{{Kofun navbar}}

The iron armor and other burial goods are stored at Osaka University and were designation1 as {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property}} of Japan in 2015.<ref>"Osaka City Art Museum Registered as Cultural Property," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' March 15, 2015.</ref>
Additionally, a 1964 color film documenting the excavation was rediscovered in 2009.<ref>"1964 Nonaka Kofun Excavation Film Found," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' December 24, 2012.</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:野中古墳 (藤井寺市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:野中古墳 (藤井寺市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:野中古墳 (藤井寺市)]]
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Nonaka Kofun | native_name = 野中古墳 | location = {{ill|Fujiidera|WD=Q634011|1=Fujiidera}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.56025|N|135.60258333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | region = Japan | type = {{ill|Square tumulus (Hōfun)|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square tumulus (Hōfun)|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | length = 37m per side | built = 5th century | burial = Wooden coffin, wooden chest | burial goods = Armor, iron weapons | designation1 = National [[Historic Site of Japan|Historic Site]] (part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|en|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|WD=Q4250076|lt=Furuichi Kofun Group|1=Furuichi Kofun Cluster}}) | map = Japan Osaka | map_caption = location in Osaka Prefecture | image = {{image requested|cat=Fujiidera, Osaka}} | architectural_styles= {{ill|square kofun|WD=Q11504353|1=Square Kofun}} | part_of= {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|WD=Q4250076|1=Furuichi Kofun Cluster}} }} '''Nonaka Kofun''' (野中古墳) is a mid-[[Kofun period]] Kofun ([[kofun]]) located in {{ill|Fujiidera|en|Fujiidera|WD=Q634011|lt=Fujidera|1=Fujiidera}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}, Japan. A significant number of iron weapons, armor, and {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} have been excavated from the site. The kofun is designation1 as a part of the {{ill|UNESCO|en|UNESCO|WD=Q7809|lt=UNESCO|1=UNESCO}} [[World Historic Site]] "Ancient Kofun Clusters," specifically within the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|en|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|WD=Q4250076|lt=Furuichi Kofun Cluster|1=Furuichi Kofun Cluster}}. It is also a [[National Historic Site of Japan]].
== Overview == The Nonaka Kofun is part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|en|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|WD=Q4250076|lt=Furuichi Kofun Cluster|1=Furuichi Kofun Cluster}} and is situated north of the rear circle of the {{ill|Makasuyama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳|1=Hakayama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makasuyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakayama Kofun|qq=}}, leading to speculation that it may have served as a {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|subsidiary Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baizuka|qq=}} for the latter.<ref name="yomiuri140212">"Restoration Completed for Nonaka Kofun Armor," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' February 12, 2014.</ref>
The mound is a square-shaped tumulus (方墳) built in two tiers, with each side measuring 37 meters and a height of 4 meters. It features a surrounding moat, {{ill|Fukiishi|en|Fukiishi|WD=Q11621213|lt=fukiishi|1=Fukiishi}} (roofing stones), cylindrical haniwa rows, and figurative haniwa, including helmets, "Kinugasa" lids, and horned helmets (衝角付冑). Numerous {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}} were excavated from the mound's summit.<ref name="yomiuri140212"/>
Beneath the mound's summit, five sets of burial facilities were aligned in parallel, consisting of wooden coffins and wooden chests. Only the second wooden coffin is believed to have contained human remains, while the other four are interpreted as dedicated for grave goods. These facilities yielded a significant collection of grave goods, including: * 11 sets of iron armor ([[tankō]] 短甲) * 11 helmets (冑) * Components such as grass skirts and shoulder guards * Iron swords (鉄刀), iron spears (鉄剣), and a large number of iron arrowheads (鉄鏃).
The abundant iron burial goods, Sue pottery, and weapons provide invaluable insights into the burial practices of the mid-Kofun period. The tumulus is estimated to have been built during the early to mid-5th century.
== Excavations and Preservation == In 1964, an excavation led by [[:en:Osaka University|Osaka University]] unearthed approximately 2,000 burial goods.<ref>"Crowdfunding Advances for 3D Digitalization of Important burial goods," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' December 22, 2018.</ref> The site was designation1 as a National Historic Site in 1995 and incorporated into the unified designation of the "Furuichi Kofun Cluster" in 2001. The iron armor and other burial goods are stored at Osaka University and were designation1 as {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}} of Japan in 2015.<ref>"Osaka City Art Museum Registered as Cultural Property," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' March 15, 2015.</ref>
Additionally, a 1964 color film documenting the excavation was rediscovered in 2009.<ref>"1964 Nonaka Kofun Excavation Film Found," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' December 24, 2012.</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}

== External Links == * [http://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/10kakuka/34shakaikyoiku/03bunkazai/04isekishokai/13kaisetsu/k_nonaka.html Nonaka Kofun] (Habikino City Website) * [http://www.let.osaka-u.ac.jp/kouko/nonaka/ Nonaka Kofun] (Osaka University Archaeology Laboratory)

The kofun is designation1 as a part of the {{ill|UNESCO|en|UNESCO|WD=Q7809|lt=UNESCO|1=UNESCO}} [[World Historic Site]] "Ancient Kofun Clusters," specifically within the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|en|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|WD=Q4250076|lt=Furuichi Kofun Cluster|1=Furuichi Kofun Cluster}}. It is also a [[National Historic Site of Japan]].
== Overview == The Nonaka Kofun is part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|en|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|WD=Q4250076|lt=Furuichi Kofun Cluster|1=Furuichi Kofun Cluster}} and is situated north of the rear circle of the {{ill|Makasuyama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳|1=Hakayama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makasuyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakayama Kofun|qq=}}, leading to speculation that it may have served as a {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|subsidiary Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baizuka|qq=}} for the latter.<ref name="yomiuri140212">"Restoration Completed for Nonaka Kofun Armor," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' February 12, 2014.</ref>
The mound is a square-shaped tumulus (方墳) built in two tiers, with each side measuring 37 meters and a height of 4 meters. It features a surrounding moat, {{ill|Fukiishi|en|Fukiishi|WD=Q11621213|lt=fukiishi|1=Fukiishi}} (roofing stones), cylindrical haniwa rows, and figurative haniwa, including helmets, "Kinugasa" lids, and horned helmets (衝角付冑). Numerous {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}} were excavated from the mound's summit.<ref name="yomiuri140212"/>
Beneath the mound's summit, five sets of burial facilities were aligned in parallel, consisting of wooden coffins and wooden chests. Only the second wooden coffin is believed to have contained human remains, while the other four are interpreted as dedicated for grave goods. These facilities yielded a significant collection of grave goods, including: * 11 sets of iron armor ([[tankō]] 短甲) * 11 helmets (冑) * Components such as grass skirts and shoulder guards * Iron swords (鉄刀), iron spears (鉄剣), and a large number of iron arrowheads (鉄鏃).
The abundant iron burial goods, Sue pottery, and weapons provide invaluable insights into the burial practices of the mid-Kofun period. The tumulus is estimated to have been built during the early to mid-5th century.
== Excavations and Preservation == In 1964, an excavation led by [[:en:Osaka University|Osaka University]] unearthed approximately 2,000 burial goods.<ref>"Crowdfunding Advances for 3D Digitalization of Important burial goods," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' December 22, 2018.</ref> The site was designation1 as a National Historic Site in 1995 and incorporated into the unified designation of the "Furuichi Kofun Cluster" in 2001. The iron armor and other burial goods are stored at Osaka University and were designation1 as {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}} of Japan in 2015.<ref>"Osaka City Art Museum Registered as Cultural Property," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' March 15, 2015.</ref>
Additionally, a 1964 color film documenting the excavation was rediscovered in 2009.<ref>"1964 Nonaka Kofun Excavation Film Found," ''Yomiuri Shimbun,'' December 24, 2012.</ref>
